main_content:
Steps - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupStepsInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/StepsMore actionsStepsYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Feb 04, 20252 min read StepDescriptionsCommentsStepDescriptionsCommentsRequired Software                               Ubuntu 20 O/SMysql 8.0.20     MongoDB 3.4            Java 8      STS (Spring Tool Suite)       Jetty Server (inside the STS tool)                                                                                   2. Create a required folders                                   /app/    /backup/   /install/   /install/framework/   /install/framework/build/      mkdir -p    /app/    /backup/   /install/   /install/framework/   /install/framework/build/                                                                           3. Import meta from gitUse deploy_framework.sh script for import meta on mongod from git framework-web/deploy/bin folder   cd gitpath/framework-web/deploy/bin./install_framework.sh This script is used to install Application . It provides options for specifying the Database name host name port number mongodb username, mongodb password, application name ../install_framework.sh -h
Mandatory Options for application:
   -o,  --organization         Organization names (comma-separated)
   -a,  --app-name             Application name
   -d,  --mongo-database       MongoDB Database name
   -u,  --username             Database username
   -p,  --mongo-password       Mongo password
   -D,  --default-app          Set 'no' if the common app is not required (default: yes)
   -S,  --server               Select machine type it's a server or local(default: server)
   -H,  --host                 MongoDB host name (default: localhost)
   -P,  --port                 MongoDB port number (default: 27017) 4. Import data from gitUse create_all_db.sh script for import data on mysql from /app/framework/bin/ folder   cd /app/framework/bin/create_all_db.shThis script is used to create a mysql databases and tables. It provides options for specifying the Database type , schema name, username password .Usage: ./create_all_db.sh -h
Options:
*    -o, --org               Inferyx platform organization name
*    -a, --app               Inferyx platform application name
*    -s, --schema-type       Database schema name with or without (comma seperated) like: admin,cfa or all
*    -t, --database-type     Database type, ex: mysql/sql/mssql/snowflake
*    -u, --username          Mysql/Snowflake username
*    -p, --password          Mysql password (Required if database type mysql)
     -nu, --username         Neo4j username
     -np, --password         Neo4j password
     -nd, --neo4j_database   Neo4j database
*    -ai, --acc              Snowflake New account id
*    -r, --role              Snowflake account role
*    -fn, --first-name       Snowflake account first name
*    -ln, --last-name        Snowflake account last name
*    -e, --email-id          Snowflake account email id
*    -R, --resion            Snowflake account resion
*    -E, --edition           Snowflake account edition
*    -D, --database          Snowflake database name.(Required if database type snowflake)
     -c, --code              Application code (Only required if run from API)
     -i, --app-id            Application id (Only required if run from API)
*    -A, --account           Snowflake account number.(Required if database type snowflake)
*    -w, --warehouse         Snowflake warehouse name.(Required if database type snowflake)
     -h, --help              Display this help message 5    Import license file    /app/framework/license/                   (license file provided by Inferyx DevOps team)                                                    6   Import project inside the STS tool         Goto import projectadd maven project , select project path 7    Configure Java patch                             Right click on framework-app, then click Build Path and configure Java JRE path     8    Update the project                          Right click on framework-app, then click Maven and select Update Project           9    Run the project                                  Right click on framework-web, then click Run with Jetty    , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapsePre-requisitesPre-requisitesInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisBackup Recovery ProcessBackup Recovery ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisTech StackTech StackInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisProject White-lable ProcessProject White-lable ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInferyx Project SetupInferyx Project SetupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Inferyx Project Setup - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Inferyx Project Setup - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Inferyx Project SetupMore actionsInferyx Project SetupYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Feb 20, 20251 min read1. Create the Inferyx User and Directories Create the home directory for the user:sudo mkdir /home/inferyxCreate a user group and add a new user:sudo addgroup inferyx 
sudo useradd inferyx -g inferyxSet the password for the inferyx user:sudo passwd inferyxSet the default shell to /bin/bash:sudo chsh -s /bin/bash inferyxSet ownership of the /home/inferyx directory:sudo chown inferyx:inferyx /home/inferyx2. Configure Sudo Access for the Inferyx UserTo allow the inferyx user to execute administrative commands, edit the /etc/sudoers file:Open the sudoers file:sudo nano /etc/sudoersAdd the following line to grant sudo privileges to the inferyx user:inferyx ALL=(ALL:ALL) ALLSwitch to the Inferyx UserOnce the inferyx user has been created, switch to the inferyx user to ensure all subsequent tasks are performed under the correct user context. Run the following command:sudo su - inferyx3. Create Required DirectoriesThe following directories are essential for storing application data, backups, and installation files.Create the necessary directories using the mkdir -p command:sudo mkdir -p /app/ /backup/ /install/ /install/framework/ /install/framework/build/Set directory permissions:Full permissions (777) for /backup and /install:sudo chmod -R 777 /backup /install/Restricted permissions (744) for /app:sudo chmod -R 744 /app/Change ownership of all directories to the inferyx user:sudo chown inferyx:inferyx /backup /install/ /app/Set  Server timezone:sudo timedatectl set-timezone IST4. Install Software PackagesTo run the Inferyx Platform, the following software components are required. These applications must be installed and configured in the environment to ensure smooth operation.Java Development Kit (JDK)Version: 1.8 ,11, 17 Purpose: Below is the following link to install JavaInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21 Apache TomcatVersion: 9.0.83 or laterPurpose: Below is the following link to install Apache Tomcat .Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0 MySQLVersion: 8.0.20 or laterPurpose: Below is the following link to install MySQL.Install and Configure Mysql MongoDBVersion: 3.4.7 or laterPurpose: Below is the following link to install MongoDB.Install and Configure Mongo PythonVersion: 3.7 , 3.8.10 or later Purpose: Below is the following link to install Python .Install and Configure Python (Optional) Neo4jVersion: 5.16.0 or later Purpose: Below is the following link to install Neo4j .Install and Configure Neo4J Camunda BPMVersion: 7.21.0 or later Purpose: Below is the following link to install Camunda BPM.Install and Configure Camunda  This setup ensures that all  software dependencies for the Inferyx platform are met, with  components available for extended functionality.     , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall/update inferyx platformInstall/update inferyx platformInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisProject  Deployment ProcessProject  Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure Neo4JInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisInstall and Configure MongoInstall and Configure MongoInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Install and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21 - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21 - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21More actionsInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: May 20, 20251 min readInstalling the  JRE/JDKThe easiest option for installing Java is to use the version packaged with Ubuntu.To install this version, first update the package index:sudo apt updateYou can install Java 8 on Ubuntu 22.04/20.04/18.04/16.04 from OpenJDK binaries available on the default upstream repositories. Run the command:sudo apt install openjdk-8-jdkVerify the installation with:java -versionYou’ll see the following output:openjdk version "1.8.0_312"
OpenJDK Runtime Environment (build 1.8.0_312-8u312-b07-0ubuntu1~20.04-b07)
OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM (build 25.312-b07, mixed mode)Install Java 11 and Java 17 Update Package IndexBefore installing Java, update the package index:sudo apt updateSince you already have Java 8, install Java 11 and 17 using:sudo apt install openjdk-11-jdk -y 
sudo apt install openjdk-17-jdk -y
sudo apt install openjdk-21-jdk -yVerify Installed Java Versionsupdate-alternatives --list javaYou should see paths similar to:/usr/lib/jvm/java-8-openjdk-amd64/jre/bin/java 
/usr/lib/jvm/java-11-openjdk-amd64/bin/java 
/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64/bin/java
/usr/lib/jvm/java-21-openjdk-amd64/bin/javaSwitch Between Java Versionssudo update-alternatives --config javaYou'll see an output like this:There are 3 choices for the alternative java (providing /usr/bin/java).
  Selection    Path                                            Priority   Status
------------------------------------------------------------
  0            /usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64/bin/java     1711      auto mode
  1            /usr/lib/jvm/java-8-openjdk-amd64/bin/java      1081      manual mode
  2            /usr/lib/jvm/java-11-openjdk-amd64/bin/java     1111      manual mode
  3            /usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64/bin/java     1711      manual mode
  4            /usr/lib/jvm/java-21-openjdk-amd64/bin/java     2111      manual mode
Press <enter> to keep the current choice[*], or type selection number: Enter the selection number for the Java version you want to use.  , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure Neo4JInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisInstallation ChecklistInstallation ChecklistInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisInstall and configure Docker ContainerInstall and configure Docker ContainerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisRelease Notes - 6.2.5.8Release Notes - 6.2.5.8Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0 - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0 - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0More actionsInstall and Configure Tomcat 9.0Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Oct 16, 20241 min readFirst, navigate to the /tmp directory:cd /tmpDownload the archive using wget by running the following command:sudo wget https://archive.apache.org/dist/tomcat/tomcat-9/v9.0.83/bin/apache-tomcat-9.0.83.tar.gzThen, extract the archive you downloaded by running:sudo tar -xvf apache-tomcat-9.0.83.tar.gz
sudo mv apache-tomcat-9.0.83 /opt/tomcatSince you have already created a user, you can now grant inferyx ownership over the extracted installation by running:sudo chown -R inferyx:inferyx /opt/tomcat
sudo chmod -R u+x /opt/tomcatYou’ll store the tomcat service in a file named tomcat, under /etc/init.d/. Create the file for editing by running:sudo nano /etc/init.d/tomcatAdd the following lines: /etc/init.d/tomcat#!/bin/sh
export CATALINA_HOME="/opt/tomcat"
export JAVA_HOME="/usr/lib/jvm/java-1.8.0-openjdk-1.8.0.332.b09-2.el8_6.x86_64/jre"
RETVAL=$?
case $1 in
start)
    if [ -f $CATALINA_HOME/bin/startup.sh ];
    then
        echo $"Starting Tomcat"
        su -p -s /bin/sh inferyx $CATALINA_HOME/bin/startup.sh
    fi
    ;;
stop)
    if [ -f $CATALINA_HOME/bin/shutdown.sh ];
    then
        echo $"Stopping Tomcat"
        su -p -s /bin/sh inferyx $CATALINA_HOME/bin/shutdown.sh
    fi
    ;;
*)
    echo $"Usage: $0 {start|stop}"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac
exit $RETVALModify the highlighted value of JAVA_HOME and Tomcat if it differs from the one you noted previously.When you’re done, save and close the file.You’ll store the tomcat service in a file named tomcat.service, under /etc/systemd/system. Create the file for editing by running:sudo nano /etc/systemd/system/tomcat.serviceAdd the following lines: /etc/systemd/system/tomcat.service[Unit]
Description=Apache Tomcat Web Application
After=network.target
[Service]
Type=forking
Environment=CATALINA_PID=/opt/tomcat/temp/tomcat.pid
Environment=CATALINA_HOME=/opt/tomcat
Environment=CATALINA_BASE=/opt/tomcat
ExecStart=/opt/tomcat/bin/startup.sh
ExecStop=/opt/tomcat/bin/shutdown.sh
User=inferyx
Group=inferyx
UMask=0007
RestartSec=10
Restart=always
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.targetWhen you’re done, save and close the file.Reload the systemd daemon so that it becomes aware of the new service:sudo systemctl daemon-reloadNavigate to Tomcat's bin directory:cd /opt/tomcat/binCreate or edit the setenv.sh file:nano setenv.shAdd memory configurations to the setenv.sh file:CATALINA_OPTS="-Xms4g -Xmx8g"
Environment=JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-8-openjdk-amd64Modify the highlighted value of JAVA_HOME if it differs from the one you noted previously.Save and exitEnsure the setenv.sh file is executable:chmod +x setenv.shAfter these steps, Tomcat will use the specified memory options when started.You can then start the Tomcat service by typing:sudo systemctl start tomcat 
 , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure Neo4JInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisInstall and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MysqlInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure CamundaInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstallation ChecklistInstallation ChecklistInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure MongoInstall and Configure MongoInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Install and Configure Tomcat 10 - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install and Configure Tomcat 10 - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install and Configure Tomcat 10More actionsInstall and Configure Tomcat 10Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaMay 20, 20251 min readNavigate to the /tmp directory for temporary file handling.cd /tmp sudo wget https://archive.apache.org/dist/tomcat/tomcat-10/v10.1.39/bin/apache-tomcat-10.1.39.tar.gz Download Tomcat 10.1.39 archive from the official Apache archive.sudo tar -xvf apache-tomcat-10.1.39.tar.gz Extract the downloaded .tar.gz archive.sudo mv apache-tomcat-10.1.39 /opt/tomcat Move the extracted folder to /opt, renaming it to tomcat.2. Set Ownership and Permissionssudo chown -R inferyx:inferyx /opt/tomcat Assign ownership of the Tomcat directory to user inferyx.sudo chmod -R u+x /opt/tomcat Ensure the user has execute permissions on all files.3. Create Legacy Init Script (Optional)sudo nano /etc/init.d/tomcat Create and open a legacy init script.#!/bin/sh
export CATALINA_HOME="/opt/tomcat"
export JAVA_HOME="/usr/lib/jvm/java-21-openjdk-amd64"
RETVAL=$?
case $1 in
start)
    if [ -f $CATALINA_HOME/bin/startup.sh ]; then
        echo $"Starting Tomcat"
        su -p -s /bin/sh inferyx $CATALINA_HOME/bin/startup.sh
    fi
    ;;
stop)
    if [ -f $CATALINA_HOME/bin/shutdown.sh ]; then
        echo $"Stopping Tomcat"
        su -p -s /bin/sh inferyx $CATALINA_HOME/bin/shutdown.sh
    fi
    ;;
*)
    echo $"Usage: $0 {start|stop}"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac
exit $RETVAL
4. Create systemd Servicesudo nano /etc/systemd/system/tomcat.service Open systemd service file for Tomcat.[Unit]
Description=Apache Tomcat Web Application
After=network.target
[Service]
Type=forking
Environment=CATALINA_PID=/opt/tomcat/temp/tomcat.pid
Environment=CATALINA_HOME=/opt/tomcat
Environment=CATALINA_BASE=/opt/tomcat
ExecStart=/opt/tomcat/bin/startup.sh
ExecStop=/opt/tomcat/bin/shutdown.sh
User=inferyx
Group=inferyx
UMask=0007
RestartSec=10
Restart=always
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
5. Reload systemd and Enable Tomcatsudo systemctl daemon-reload Reload systemd to recognize the new service file.sudo systemctl enable tomcat Enable the Tomcat service to start on boot.sudo systemctl start tomcat Start the Tomcat service.6. Create setenv.sh for Environment Configurationcd /opt/tomcat/bin Navigate to Tomcat's bin directory.sudo nano setenv.sh Create the setenv.sh file to configure environment variables.#!/bin/bash
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-21-openjdk-amd64
export CATALINA_OPTS="-Xms4g -Xmx8g -Dapp.base.dir=/app/framework"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS --add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS --add-opens=java.base/java.io=ALL-UNNAMED"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS --add-opens=java.base/java.util=ALL-UNNAMED"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS --add-opens=java.base/java.util.concurrent=ALL-UNNAMED"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS --add-opens=java.rmi/sun.rmi.transport=ALL-UNNAMED"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS --add-opens=java.base/java.time=ALL-UNNAMED"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS --add-opens=java.base/sun.nio.ch=ALL-UNNAMED"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS --add-opens=java.base/java.lang.invoke=ALL-UNNAMED"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS --add-opens=java.base/java.nio=ALL-UNNAMED"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS --add-opens=java.base/sun.util.calendar=ALL-UNNAMED"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS -agentlib:jdwp=transport=dt_socket,server=y,suspend=n,address=*:8000"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS -Dframework.property=/app/framework/conf/framework.properties"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS -Dlogback.configurationFile=/opt/tomcat/conf/log4j2.xml"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS -Dframework.log.path=/app/framework/logs"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS -Dframework.config=/app/framework/conf/framework.properties"
export CLASSPATH=/opt/tomcat/bin/bootstrap.jar:/opt/tomcat/bin/tomcat-juli.jar
CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS -Djava.class.path.print=true"
echo "CLASSPATH: $CLASSPATH"
sudo chmod +x setenv.sh Make setenv.sh executable.7. Verify Tomcatsudo systemctl status tomcat Check if Tomcat is running properly.tail -f /opt/tomcat/logs/catalina.out View live logs for troubleshooting. , multiple selections available,Atlassian Intelligence

title: Install and Configure Mysql - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install and Configure Mysql - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install and Configure MysqlMore actionsInstall and Configure MysqlYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Feb 20, 20251 min readFirst, navigate to the /tmp directory:cd /tmpDownload the archive using wget by running the following command:wget https://cdn.mysql.com/archives/mysql-8.0/mysql-server_8.0.20-2ubuntu20.04_amd64.deb-bundle.tarOR For Ubuntu 22 use this link other steps are same sudo wget https://cdn.mysql.com/archives/mysql-8.0/mysql-server_8.0.33-1ubuntu22.04_amd64.deb-bundle.tar
tar -xvf mysql-server_8.0.33-1ubuntu22.04_amd64.deb-bundle.tarThen, extract the archive you downloaded by running:tar -xvf mysql-server_8.0.20-2ubuntu20.04_amd64.deb-bundle.tarNow you can use dpkg to begin the install:sudo dpkg -i  *.debDuring installation,  facing any error. Then, execute the quick fix commands:sudo apt --fix-broken installAnd then  you’ll be prompted with a configuration screen where you can set the username password.You’ll configure the my.cnf   file , under /etc/mysql/my.cnf. Update  the file for editing by running:sudo nano /etc/mysql/my.cnfAdd the following lines end of files: /etc/mysql/my.cnf[mysqld]
secure-file-priv = ""
local-infile=1
optimizer_search_depth=0
default_time_zone="+04:00"
[mysql]
local-infile=1
[client]
loose-local-infile=1
bind-address = 0.0.0.0Now you can use mysql to begin the open MYSQL console:mysql -u root -pNow create a user  inferyx   and set password:SET GLOBAL local_infile = 1;
  CREATE USER 'inferyx'@'localhost' IDENTIFIED WITH mysql_native_password BY 'inferyx';
  GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON * . * TO 'inferyx'@'localhost' with grant option;
  CREATE USER 'inferyx'@'%' IDENTIFIED WITH mysql_native_password BY 'inferyx';
  GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON * . * TO 'inferyx'@'%' with grant option;
  FLUSH PRIVILEGES; , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure Neo4JInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisInstall and Configure MongoInstall and Configure MongoInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisInstallation ChecklistInstallation ChecklistInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Install and Configure Mongo - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install and Configure Mongo - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install and Configure MongoMore actionsInstall and Configure MongoYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Oct 21, 20242 min readFirst, navigate to the /tmp directory:cd /optDownload the archive using wget by running the following command:wget https://fastdl.mongodb.org/linux/mongodb-linux-x86_64-3.4.7.tgzThen, extract and rename the archive you downloaded by running:sudo tar -xvf mongodb-linux-x86_64-3.4.7.tgz
sudo mv mongodb-linux-x86_64-3.4.7 mongodbnext, Create the db directory to the / (root) directory:sudo mkdir -p /data/dbSince you have already created a user, you can now grant inferyx ownership over the extracted installation by running:sudo chown -R inferyx:inferyx /opt/mongodb
sudo chown -R inferyx:inferyx /data/db
sudo chmod -R u+x /opt/mongodb
sudo chmod -R u+x /data/dbYou’ll store the mongodb service in a file named mongodb.service, under /etc/systemd/system/. Create the file for editing by running:sudo nano /etc/systemd/system/mongodb.serviceAdd the following lines: /etc/systemd/system#Unit contains the dependencies to be satisfied before the service is started.
[Unit]
Description=MongoDB Database
After=network.target
Documentation=https://docs.mongodb.org/manual
# Service tells systemd, how the service should be started.
# Key `User` specifies that the server will run under the mongodb user and
# `ExecStart` defines the startup command for MongoDB server.
[Service]
User=inferyx
Group=inferyx
ExecStart=/opt/mongodb/bin/mongod --quiet --config /etc/mongod.conf
# Install tells systemd when the service should be automatically started.
# `multi-user.target` means the server will be automatically started during boot.
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target Grant inferyx ownership over the extracted installation by running:sudo chown -R inferyx:inferyx /etc/systemd/system/mongodb.service
sudo chmod -R u+x /etc/systemd/system/mongodb.serviceNow create the mongodb configure file under /etc/. Create the file for editing by running:sudo nano /etc/mongod.confAdd the following lines in : /etc/mongod.conf# mongod.conf
# for documentation of all options, see:
#   http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/reference/configuration-options/
# Where and how to store data.
storage:
  dbPath: /opt/mongodb
  journal:
    enabled: true
#  engine:
#  mmapv1:
#  wiredTiger:
# where to write logging data.
systemLog:
  destination: file
  logAppend: true
  path: /var/log/mongodb/mongod.log
# network interfaces
net:
  port: 27017
  bindIp: 127.0.0.1
#processManagement:
#security:
    #authorization: enabled
#operationProfiling:
#replication:
#sharding:
## Enterprise-Only Options:
#auditLog:
#snmp:Now create the mongodb folder under /var/log/:sudo mkdir mongodb
sudo mkdir -p /var/log/mongodbThen create the mongod.log log file under /var/log/mongodb/:sudo touch /var/log/mongodb/mongod.logGrant inferyx ownership over the extracted installation by running:sudo chown -R inferyx:inferyx /var/log/mongodb/mongod.log
sudo chmod -R u+x /var/log/mongodb/mongod.logGrant inferyx ownership over the extracted installation by running:sudo chown -R inferyx:inferyx /etc/mongod.conf
sudo chmod -R u+x /etc/mongod.confAdd the mongodb  to your path:export PATH=$PATH:/opt/mongodb/binUpdate your pathsudo nano ~/.bashrc
export PATH=$PATH:/opt/mongodb/binopen the .bashrc  file and and add the mongodb pathsource ~/.bashrcStart mongodb serversudo systemctl start mongodbNow Open the mongo console and Create Admin Usermongo
use admindb.createUser({
    user: "admin",
    pwd: "20Admin19",
    roles: ["root"]
});Now Open the mongo console and choose admin database for create the user:mongo
use adminNow create a user username and set password:db.createUser(
 {
   user: "inferyx",
   pwd: "password",
    "roles" : [
       {
           "role" : "read",
           "db" : "framework"
       },
       {
           "role" : "readWrite",
           "db" : "framework"
       },
       {
           "role" : "dbAdmin",
           "db" : "framework"
       },
   ]
 }
);Finally enable the security option from mongo config file: Open the /etc/mongod.conf by running: sudo nano /etc/mongod.conf#security:
    #authorization: enabled
    To
security:
    authorization: enabledNow Restart  mongodb serversudo systemctl restart mongodb , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure Neo4JInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall/update inferyx platformInstall/update inferyx platformInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Install or Upgrade Mongo 7.x - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install or Upgrade Mongo 7.x - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install or Upgrade Mongo 7.xMore actionsInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Apr 25, 20252 min readBefore installing MongoDB 7.x, ensure that you take a dump of the existing MongoDB data, then stop any existing MongoDB  then proceed with the new installation.Step : Take Backup of MongoDB DatabaseBefore stopping MongoDB version 3.4.7, take a backup of the framework database.Navigate to the backup directory:cd /backupRun the mongodump command to create a backup:mongodump --host=localhost --port=27017 --db framework  --username=admin --password=20Admin19 --authenticationDatabase=adminStep : Create a  Tar File tar -czvf dump.tar.gz dumpAfter creating the tar file, install MongoDB 7.Once MongoDB 7 is installed, restore the MongoDB dump.The MongoDB restore steps are given below. Stop Mongo 3.4.7 And Install Mongo 7.x1.Verify MongoDB Service is running or notsudo systemctl status mongodb2. Stop MongoDB Service (if not already stopped)sudo systemctl stop mongodb3. Verify MongoDB Service is Stoppedsudo systemctl status mongodb4. Backup MongoDB Directory cd /opt/
sudo mv mongodb mongodb_bkp5. Backup MongoDB Configuration Filecd /etc/
sudo mv mongod.conf mongod_bkp.conf6. Backup MongoDB Service Filecd /etc/systemd/system/
sudo mv mongodb.service mongodb_bkp.service7. Reload Systemd Daemon (to apply changes)sudo systemctl daemon-reload8.Check and backup storage data inOpen the MongoDB config file and check the dbPath.cat /etc/mongod.conf Look for the section:storage:   dbPath: /var/lib/mongodbIf /var/lib/mongodb is the same path then take backup .mv /var/lib/mongodb /var/lib/mongodb_bkp Install Mongo 7.xUpdate System Packagessudo apt updateRemove any existing mongoDB GPG KeyTo avoid conflicts, remove any outdated or existing GPG key for mongoDB:sudo rm -f /usr/share/keyrings/mongodb-org-7.0.gpgImport the MongoDB 7.0 GPG Keycurl -fsSL https://pgp.mongodb.com/server-7.0.asc | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/mongodb-org-7.0.gpgAdd the MongoDB Repositoryecho "deb [ arch=amd64,arm64 signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/mongodb-org-7.0.gpg ] https://repo.mongodb.org/apt/ubuntu $(lsb_release -cs)/mongodb-org/7.0 multiverse" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/mongodb-org-7.0.listUpdate the Package Indexsudo apt updateInstall MongoDBsudo apt install -y mongodb-orgSet Correct Permissionssudo chown -R mongodb:mongodb /var/lib/mongodb /var/log/mongodb
sudo chmod -R 755 /var/lib/mongodb /var/log/mongodbStart the MongoDB Servicesudo systemctl start mongod Check the MongoDB Service Statussudo systemctl status mongod Access MongoDB ShellmongoshCreate Administrative UsersSwitch to the admin Databaseuse adminCreate an Admin Userdb.createUser({
    user: "admin",
    pwd: "20Admin19",
    roles: ["root"]
});Create an application user switch to the framework database and create the user:db.createUser({
    user: "inferyx",
    pwd: "20Inferyx19",
    roles: [
        { role: "read", db: "framework" },
        { role: "readWrite", db: "framework" },
        { role: "dbAdmin", db: "framework" }
    ]
}); Update MongoDB Bind IP If Needed To allow remote connections to MongoDB:Edit the MongoDB configuration file:sudo nano /etc/mongod.confIn the net section, change bindIp from 127.0.0.1 to 0.0.0.0 and enable security net:
  port: 27017
  bindIp: 0.0.0.0
#security:
    #authorization: enabled
    To
security:
    authorization: enabledSave the file and restart MongoDB:sudo systemctl restart mongodStep : Restore the Mongo Backup Log in to the target server and navigate to the backup directory:cd /backupRun the mongodump command to create a backup:Extract the compressed backup file:tar -xvf dump.tar.gzNavigate to the extracted dump directory:cd dump/Restore the database using mongorestore:mongorestore --host=localhost --port=27017 --db framework --username=admin --password=20Admin19 --authenticationDatabase=admin /backup/dump/framework, multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall and Configure MongoInstall and Configure MongoInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall/update inferyx platformInstall/update inferyx platformInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisRelease Notes - 6.2.2.2Release Notes - 6.2.2.2Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisRelease Notes - 5.4.8.2Release Notes - 5.4.8.2Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Install and Configure Neo4J - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install and Configure Neo4J - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install and Configure Neo4JMore actionsInstall and Configure Neo4JYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Feb 14, 20251 min readCreate Directory for Neo4jcd /opt/ Download the Neo4j Enterprise tarball:cd /opt/neo4j
sudo wget https://dist.neo4j.org/neo4j-enterprise-5.16.0-unix.tar.gzExtract the downloaded tarball:sudo tar -xvzf neo4j-enterprise-5.16.0-unix.tar.gz Move the extracted files to the Neo4j directory:#remove this 
sudo mv neo4j-enterprise-5.16.0-unix /opt/neo4jEnsure proper ownership:sudo chown -R inferyx:inferyx /opt/neo4j
sudo chmod +x /opt/neo4jCreate the /app/neo4j/config/ directory (new step)sudo mkdir -p /app/neo4j/config/Accept Neo4j LicenseRun the following command to accept the Neo4j evaluation license:sudo /opt/neo4j/bin/neo4j-admin server license --accept-evaluationCreate a systemd service file to manage Neo4j:sudo nano /etc/systemd/system/neo4j.serviceAdd the following content to the file:[Unit]
Description=Neo4j Graph Database
After=network.target
[Service]
User=inferyx
Group=inferyx
ExecStart=/opt/neo4j/bin/neo4j start
ExecStop=/opt/neo4j/bin/neo4j stop
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=10
LimitNOFILE=60000
TimeoutSec=120
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target Reload Systemd and Enable Neo4j Servicesudo systemctl daemon-reload 
sudo systemctl enable neo4jConfigure Neo4jCreate the directory and Configuration file for APOC configuration:sudo mkdir -p /opt/neo4j/conf/ 
sudo touch /opt/neo4j/conf/apoc.confEdit the apoc.conf file:sudo nano /opt/neo4j/conf/apoc.confAdd the following content:apoc.import.file.enabled=true
apoc.uuid.enabled=true
apoc.ttl.enabled=false
apoc.export.file.enabled=trueUpdate Neo4j ConfigurationEdit the Neo4j configuration file:sudo nano /opt/neo4j/conf/neo4j.confUpdate or add the following settings:# Set the IP address Neo4j listens on
dbms.default_listen_address=0.0.0.0
# Advertise Neo4j on a specific IP
dbms.default_advertised_address=52.200.177.119
# Change the HTTP connector port (default is 7474)
dbms.connector.http.listen_address=:7474
# Change the Bolt connector port (default is 7687)
dbms.connector.bolt.listen_address=:7687
# Disable strict validation
server.config.strict_validation.enabled=false
# Allow specific procedures
dbms.security.procedures.allowlist=algo.*,apoc.*,gds.*
dbms.security.procedures.unrestricted=algo.*,apoc.*,gds.*
# Configure concurrency
gds.alpha.degree.writeConcurrency=24
gds.alpha.degree.readConcurrency=24
# Set the default listen address
server.default_listen_address=0.0.0.0Update Environment VariablesEdit the Neo4j binary files to set JAVA_HOME (if needed):sudo nano /opt/neo4j/bin/neo4j 
sudo nano /opt/neo4j/bin/neo4j-adminAdd the following line (if not already present):Scroll down to line 25 (Below REPO) and add the following line after it:export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64Start the Neo4j service:sudo systemctl start neo4jVerify that Neo4j is running by checking the service status:sudo systemctl status neo4j Verify Neo4j AccessOpen your web browser and navigate to the Neo4j web interface:http://<server_ip>:7474Replace <server_ip> with your server's IP address. , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall and Configure MongoInstall and Configure MongoInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisInstall and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MysqlInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisInstall/update inferyx platformInstall/update inferyx platformInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInferyx Project SetupInferyx Project SetupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisRelease Notes - 5.4.3.1Release Notes - 5.4.3.1Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Install and Configure Camunda - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install and Configure Camunda - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install and Configure CamundaMore actionsInstall and Configure CamundaYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Oct 18, 20243 min readFirst, navigate to the /opt directorycd /optCreate a directory where Camunda BPM will be installedsudo mkdir /opt/camundaChange the ownership of the directory to the desired user (in this case, inferyx)sudo chown -R inferyx:inferyx /opt/camunda
sudo chmod +x  /opt/camundaNavigate to the /camunda directorycd /opt/camundaDownload the Camunda BPM Tomcat Distribution To download the latest version of Camunda BPM Tomcat distribution, visit the official download page:Download Latest Camunda BPM (Platform 7)On the page, select Apache Tomcat as the distribution and download the latest version.ORDownload the Camunda BPM Tomcat using the provided linkwget https://downloads.camunda.cloud/release/camunda-bpm/tomcat/7.21/camunda-bpm-tomcat-7.21.0.tar.gzExtract the Camunda Once the download is complete, extract the .tar.gz filetar -xvzf camunda-bpm-tomcat-7.21.0.tar.gzAfter extraction, the contents will be available in the /opt/camunda directory.Change the Server PortOpen the server.xml file located in the conf directory of the extracted Camunda Tomcat installation:cd /opt/camunda/server/apache-tomcat-9.0.75/conf/Open server.xml  for editing:ls
sudo nano server.xmlFind the following line in the file<Connector port="8080" protocol="HTTP/1.1" connectionTimeout="20000" redirectPort="8443" />Change the port from 8080 to 9090<Connector port="9090" protocol="HTTP/1.1" connectionTimeout="20000" redirectPort="8443" />Change the Shutdown PortIn the server.xml file, find the Server element. It should look like this:<Server port="8005" shutdown="SHUTDOWN">Change the port attribute from 8005 to 9005:<Server port="9005" shutdown="SHUTDOWN">Save the file (Ctrl + X, then Y, and press Enter).Create a Systemd Service File for Camunda BPM 1. Create and Edit the Service Filesudo nano /etc/systemd/system/camunda.serviceAdd the Following Configuration[Unit]
Description=Camunda BPM Platform
After=network.target
[Service]
Type=forking
ExecStart=/opt/camunda/start-camunda.sh
ExecStop=/opt/camunda/shutdown-camunda.sh
User=inferyx
Group=inferyx
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5s
StartLimitIntervalSec=0
StartLimitBurst=0
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.targetSave the file and exit the editor (Ctrl + X, then Y, and press Enter).Reload Systemd and Start the Camunda ServiceReload Systemd Daemonsudo systemctl daemon-reloadStart the Camunda BPM Service sudo service camunda startEnable the Service to Start on Bootssudo service camunda enableCheck the Status of the Servicesudo service camunda statusCamunda BPM should now be running, and you can access the web interface at:http://<your-server-ip>:9090/camunda Camunda with MYSQL Configure  Log in to MySQLmysql -u your-username -pEnter your MySQL password when prompted .Create the DatabaseCREATE DATABASE framework_camunda;CREATE DATABASE: This command initiates the creation of a new database.framework_camunda: This is the name of the database you want to create. You can replace this with any other valid database name.Exit MySQLEXIT;Navigate to the conf directory:cd /opt/camunda/server/apache-tomcat-9.0.75/confOpen nano bpm-platform.xml  for editing:nano bpm-platform.xml Locate the Line to Comment Out Find the following line in the <process-engine name="default"> section:<datasource>java:jdbc/ProcessEngine</datasource>Comment Out the LineTo comment out this line, add <!-- at the beginning and --> at the end of the line. It should look like this<!-- <datasource>java:jdbc/ProcessEngine</datasource> -->This will prevent the line from being processed while keeping it in the file for future reference.Locate the Section to Add PropertiesFind the <process-engine name="default"> section or the appropriate section where properties should be added.Add Property LinesInsert the following lines inside the <properties> element. Replace database_name, username, and password with your actual database name, username, and password<property name="jdbcUrl">jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/database_name?characterEncoding=UTF-8&serverTimezone=UTC</property>
<property name="jdbcDriver">com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver</property>
<property name="jdbcUsername">username</property>
<property name="jdbcPassword">password</property>Example ConfigurationThe modified section should look something like this<process-engine name="default">
    <job-acquisition>default</job-acquisition>
    <configuration>org.camunda.bpm.engine.impl.cfg.StandaloneProcessEngineConfiguration</configuration>
    <!-- <datasource>java:jdbc/ProcessEngine</datasource> -->
    <properties>
        <property name="jdbcUrl">jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/database_name?characterEncoding=UTF-8&serverTimezone=UTC</property>
        <property name="jdbcDriver">com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver</property>
        <property name="jdbcUsername">username</property>
        <property name="jdbcPassword">password</property>
    </properties>
</process-engine>Save the file and exit the editor (Ctrl + X, then Y, and press Enter).Download MySQL Connector      Download the MySQL Connector Java .jar file from the official Maven repository:wget https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/mysql/mysql-connector-java/8.0.20/mysql-connector-java-8.0.20.jarCopy the Connector to the Required LocationsAfter downloading, copy the mysql-connector-java-8.0.20.jar file to two specific locations where Camunda and Tomcat can access it:Copy to Camunda's library folder:sudo cp mysql-connector-java-8.0.20.jar /opt/camunda/lib/Copy to Tomcat's library folder:sudo cp mysql-connector-java-8.0.20.jar /opt/camunda/server/apache-tomcat-9.0.75/lib/Start Camundasudo service start camundaThis command restarts the Camunda service, applying any configuration changes and ensuring that the service is running properly.Resolve MySQL Table Not Found IssuesIf you encounter errors related to missing MySQL tables (such as "Table not found"), you can execute the SQL scripts to create the necessary tables for the Camunda engine and identity management.Run the following SQL scripts:Create Camunda engine tables mysql -u inferyx -ppassword databases < /opt/camunda/sql/create/mysql_engine_7.21.0.sqlCreate Camunda identity tables:mysql -u username -ppassword databases < /opt/camunda/sql/create/mysql_identity_7.21.0.sqlRestart CamundaOnce the MySQL tables have been created, restart Camunda to apply the changes:sudo service restart camunda , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Inferyx Platform Documentation - title: Install and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0 - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0 - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0More actionsInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Feb 04, 20251 min readPrerequisitesDocker Installed: Ensure Docker is installed and running.docker --versionIf not installed, follow Docker installation instructions.Minikube Installed: Ensure Minikube is installed.minikube versionIf Minikube is not installed:curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/minikube/releases/latest/minikube-linux-amd64
sudo install minikube-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/minikubeAdd Docker Group: Add your user to the Docker group.sudo usermod -aG docker $USER
newgrp dockerSteps to Install and Configure Kubeflow Pipelines1. Start Minikubeminikube start --cpus=4 --memory=8192 --disk-size=30gVerify Minikube status:minikube status2. Install kubectlIf kubectl is not installed:sudo snap install kubectl --classicVerify kubectl installation:kubectl version --client3. Set the Kubeflow Pipelines VersionSet the environment variable for the Kubeflow Pipelines version:export PIPELINE_VERSION=2.0.24. Deploy Cluster-Scoped ResourcesApply the cluster-scoped resources:kubectl apply -k "github.com/kubeflow/pipelines/manifests/kustomize/cluster-scoped-resources?ref=$PIPELINE_VERSION"Wait for the resources to be established:kubectl wait --for condition=established --timeout=60s crd/applications.app.k8s.io5. Deploy Environment-Specific ResourcesApply the environment-specific manifests for development:kubectl apply -k "github.com/kubeflow/pipelines/manifests/kustomize/env/dev?ref=$PIPELINE_VERSION"Verify pods are running:kubectl get pods -A6. Update Port for the ML Pipeline UI ServicePatch the service to update the port:kubectl patch svc ml-pipeline-ui -n kubeflow --type='json' -p '[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/ports/0/port", "value": 4041}]'7. Port-Forward the ML Pipeline UIForward the ML Pipeline UI service to a local port:kubectl port-forward --address 0.0.0.0 -n kubeflow svc/ml-pipeline-ui 4042:4041Access the UI at: http://<your-minikube-ip>:4042TroubleshootingMinikube IP: Get the Minikube IP if needed:minikube ipLogs for Debugging: Check logs for specific pods if they are not running:kubectl logs -n kubeflow <pod-name>Insufficient Resources: If pods fail due to resource limits, allocate more resources to Minikube:minikube delete 
minikube start   , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall and Configure Kafka-Confluent 7.8.0Install and Configure Kafka-Confluent 7.8.0Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and configure Docker ContainerInstall and configure Docker ContainerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDocker Deployment ProcessDocker Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Genai (Optional)Install and Configure Genai (Optional)Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisKG ResultsKG ResultsInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1 - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1 - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1More actionsInstall and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Feb 20, 20251 min readFirst, navigate to the /opt directorycd /optCreate a directory where jupyterhub  will be installedsudo mkdir -p /opt/jupyterhub/
cd /opt/jupyterhub/Start by updating the system and installing necessary packages:sudo apt-get updateCheck the version of Python installed:python3 --versionInstall Node.js and npm:sudo apt-get install npm nodejsInstall pip for Python 3:sudo apt-get install python3-pipInstall JupyterHub using pip:sudo python3 -m pip install jupyterhubInstall Configurable HTTP Proxy globally using npm:sudo npm install -g configurable-http-proxyInstall Jupyter Notebook:sudo python3 -m pip install notebookGenerate the default configuration file for JupyterHub:sudo jupyterhub --generate-configThis command will create a jupyterhub_config.py file in the working directory.Edit the jupyterhub_config.py file to configure the settings for your JupyterHub instance:sudo nano jupyterhub_config.pyAdd the following configuration settings:c.JupyterHub.authenticator_class = 'jupyterhub.auth.PAMAuthenticator'
c.JupyterHub.base_url = '/jupyter'
c.JupyterHub.bind_url = 'http://0.0.0.0:8888'
c.JupyterHub.hub_bind_url = 'http://127.0.0.1:1010'
c.JupyterHub.ip = '0.0.0.0'
c.JupyterHub.port = 8888
c.Authenticator.admin_users = {'inferyx'}
c.Authenticator.allow_all = False
c.Authenticator.allowed_users = {'inferyx'}
c.Spawner.notebook_dir = '/home/{username}/'
c.Spawner.start_timeout = 120
c.LocalAuthenticator.create_system_users = True
c.ConfigurableHTTPProxy.api_url = 'http://127.0.0.1:8003'Create a service file for JupyterHub sudo nano /etc/systemd/system/jupyterhub.serviceAdd the following configuration:[Unit]
Description=JupyterHub
After=network.target
[Service]
User=root
Group=root
WorkingDirectory=/opt/jupyterhub
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/jupyterhub --config /opt/jupyterhub/jupyterhub_config.py
Restart=always
StandardOutput=append:/opt/jupyterhub/jupyterhub.log
StandardError=append:/opt/jupyterhub/jupyterhub.log
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.targetCreate the log file for JupyterHub to log its activities:sudo touch /opt/jupyterhub/jupyterhub.logMake sure the log file is writable:sudo chmod 666 /opt/jupyterhub/jupyterhub.logEnable the JupyterHub service to start on boot:sudo systemctl enable jupyterhubStart the JupyterHub service:sudo systemctl start jupyterhubCheck the status of JupyterHub:sudo systemctl status jupyterhubAccess JupyterHubhttps://<your-server-ip>:8888/jupyter  , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall Platform Python(3.9)Install Platform Python(3.9)Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall Platform Python(3.8)Install Platform Python(3.8)Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall Python 3.7Install Python 3.7Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall Python 3.8.10Install Python 3.8.10Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and configure Docker ContainerInstall and configure Docker ContainerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install Python 3.7Install Python 3.8.10Install and configure Docker ContainerInstall and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install Python 3.7More actionsInstall Python 3.7Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Oct 17, 20245 min readInstall Python 3.7 – Default MethodFirst, install the prerequisite for adding custom PPAs.sudo apt install software-properties-common -ySecond, add the deadsnakes/ppa to your APT package source list with the following command.sudo add-apt-repository ppa:deadsnakes/ppa -yOnce the repository has been imported, run an APT update to fresh your package manager to reflect the new imported PPA.sudo apt updateYou can now install Python 3.7 by executing the following code:sudo apt install python3.7 -yTo verify the installation and Python 3.7 build version, perform the following.python3.7 --versionInstall pip3 for Python 3To install pip3 (the package manager for Python 3), run the following command:sudo apt install python3-pipAfter installation, verify the pip3 installation by checking its version:pip3 --versionAfter installation upgrade pip /usr/local/bin/python3.8 -m pip install --upgrade pipInstall Python 3.7 – Manual MethodDownload the Python 3.7 archive.wget https://www.python.org/ftp/python/3.7.4/Python-3.7.4.tar.xzExtract the Python archive. Remember to change the version number if you downloaded a newer one.tar -xf Python-3.7.{version}.tar.xzOptionally, move Python 3.7 to a proper destination such as the /opt/ directory.sudo mv Python3.7.{version} /opt/Now install the dependencies required to install Python 3.7.sudo apt install build-essential zlib1g-dev libncurses5-dev libgdbm-dev libnss3-dev libssl-dev libsqlite3-dev libreadline-dev libffi-dev curl libbz2-dev make -yNavigate to the directorycd /opt/Python3.7.{version}/Now, run the ./configure –enable-optimizations command../configure --enable-optimizations --enable-sharedinstall Python binaries as follows:sudo make altinstallConfigure the dynamic linker run-time bindings:sudo ldconfig /opt/Python3.7.{version}Confirm that Python 3.7 is installed and the build version:python3.7 --versionInstall Python PIP 3.7install PIP by downloading get-pip.py using the wget command.wget https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.pyNext, install the file downloaded.python3.7 get-pip.pyOnce installed, it is a good idea to check for upgrades.python3.7 -m pip install --upgrade pipNow verify the PIP 3.7 version installed with the following command.pip3.7 --versionInstall required PIP 3.7 modules for Inferyx Platform:sudo pip3.7 install absl-py==0.15.0

SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install Python 3.7Install Python 3.8.10Install and configure Docker ContainerInstall and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install Python 3.8.10More actionsInstall Python 3.8.10Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Mar 11, 20254 min readStep 1: Update System Packages sudo apt updateStep 2: Install Dependenciessudo apt install build-essential zlib1g-dev libncurses5-dev libgdbm-dev libnss3-dev libssl-dev libreadline-dev libffi-dev libsqlite3-dev wget curl llvm libbz2-devStep 3: Download Python 3.8.10wget https://www.python.org/ftp/python/3.8.10/Python-3.8.10.tgzStep 4: Extract the Downloadtar -xf Python-3.8.10.tgz
cd Python-3.8.10              Step 5: Configure the Build Environment./configure --enable-optimizations    This command prepares the Python source code for installation and enables optimizations for better performance.Step 6: Compile the Source Codemake -j$(nproc)This command compiles the source code using the make utility. The -j$(nproc) flag tells make to use all available CPU cores for parallel compilation, speeding up the process.Step 7: Install Pythonsudo make altinstall     This command installs the compiled Python binary using sudo to gain the necessary permissions. The altinstall target prevents overwriting the default system Python.Step 8: Verify Installationpython3.8 --version      Check the installed Python version to ensure Python 3.8.10 is installed correctly.Install pip3 for Python 3To install pip3 (the package manager for Python 3), run the following command:sudo apt install python3-pipAfter installation, verify the pip3 installation by checking its version:pip3 --versionAfter installation upgrade pip/usr/local/bin/python3.8 -m pip install --upgrade pipCreating and Activating a Python Virtual Environment.Step 1: Install Python 3.8 (if not already installed):sudo apt install python3.8 python3.8-venv python3.8-devInstall Python 3.8 (if not already installed):Step 2: Install virtualenv Package (if not already installed)sudo apt install python3-virtualenvThis package is necessary for creating virtual environments.Step 3: Create the Virtual Environmentsudo python3 -m venv /opt/platformenvUse python3 -m venv to create a new virtual environment in the /opt/platformenv directoryStep 4: Set the Correct Permissions (if necessary)sudo chown -R inferyx:inferyx /opt/platformenv       Ensure that you have the appropriate permissions to create and manage files in the /opt directory. If needed, you can adjust the permissions using.Step 5: Activate the Virtual Environmentsource /opt/platformenv/bin/activate         Once the virtual environment is created, you can activate it using:Step 6: List of Python Packages to be installed.pip install absl-py==2.1.0

main_content:

SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install Python 3.7Install Python 3.8.10Install and configure Docker ContainerInstall Platform Python(3.8)Install Platform Python(3.9)Install and Configure Genai (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install Platform Python(3.8)More actionsInstall Platform Python(3.8)Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Oct 15, 2024PrerequisitesDocker installed on the host machine.Docker credentials provided by the Inferyx team (Docker Image).1. Pull the Inferyx Docker ImageRetrieve the latest version of the Docker image from the registry.docker pull inferyxhub/python_pip:1.0.02. Run the Docker ContainerUse the docker run command to start a container from the image.This option maps the container directly to the host's network. Adjust volume mappings as needed.docker run -d --restart unless-stopped --network host -it --name python_pip -v /app/framework/:/app/framework -v /usr/lib/jvm:/usr/lib/jvm -v /var/log/inferyx:/var/log/inferyx -v /var/run/mysqld:/var/run/mysqld inferyxhub/python_pip:1.0.03. Start and Stop docker containerdocker start containerid

Pull the Inferyx Docker ImageRetrieve the latest version of the Docker image from the registry.docker pull inferyxhub/python39_pip2. Run the Docker ContainerUse the docker run command to start a container from the image.This option maps the container directly to the host's network. Adjust volume mappings as needed.docker run -it --network host --name python39_pip -v /app/framework:/app/framework -v /usr/lib/jvm:/usr/lib/jvm -v /var/log/inferyx:/var/log/inferyx -v /var/run/mysqld:/var/run/mysqld inferyxhub/python39_pip:latest3.OR Run the Docker Container (Automatically)This option ensures that the container restarts automatically unless it was manually stopped. It's useful for running services that should always be available.docker run -it --network host --name python39_pip --restart unless-stopped -v /app/framework:/app/framework -v /usr/lib/jvm:/usr/lib/jvm -v /var/log/inferyx:/var/log/inferyx -v /var/run/mysqld:/var/run/mysqld inferyxhub/python39_pip:latest, multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall Platform Python(3.8)Install Platform Python(3.8)Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and configure Docker ContainerInstall and configure Docker ContainerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall/update inferyx platformInstall/update inferyx platformInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Genai (Optional)Install and Configure Genai (Optional)Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDocker Deployment ProcessDocker Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence
