title: Install and Configure Mssql (Optional) - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install and Configure Mssql (Optional) - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)More actionsInstall and Configure Mssql (Optional)Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaJul 12, 20242 min readSystem RequirementsMemory: 2 GBFile System: XFS or EXT4 (other file systems, such as BTRFS, are not supported)Disk Space: 6 GBProcessor Speed: 2 GHzProcessor Cores: 2 coresProcessor Type: x64-compatible onlyStep 1: Add a repository keyRun the following command to import the public repository GPG keys from the Microsoft website. Once asked for it, enter the password for the sudo user. If the process is successful, the output will return OK.wget -qO- https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc | sudo apt-key add -Step 2: Add a SQL Server RepositoryRun the following command to add the Microsoft SQL Server repository for SQL Server 2019. Modify the command if you are running a different version of Ubuntu.sudo add-apt-repository "$(wget -qO- https://packages.microsoft.com/config/ubuntu/18.04/mssql-server-2019.list)"Update the repository to the latest version:sudo apt-get updateStep 3: Install SQL ServerInitiate the SQL Server installation process:sudo apt-get install -y mssql-serverStep 4: Set Up SQL ServerConfigure SQL Server by running the following command:sudo /opt/mssql/bin/mssql-conf setupYou will be asked to select a SQL Server edition. Enter 2 for the Developer edition. Accept the license terms and set up a system administrator password.Step 5: Verify that SQL Server is RunningCheck the mssql service status:systemctl status mssql-serverInstall SQL Server Command-Line Tools on UbuntuStep 1: Install curlInstall curl using the following commands:sudo apt-get update
sudo apt install curlStep 2: Import Public Repository GPG KeysImport the public repository GPG keys:curl https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc | sudo apt-key add -Step 3: Register the Ubuntu RepositoryRegister the Ubuntu repository:curl https://packages.microsoft.com/config/ubuntu/20.04/prod.list | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/msprod.listStep 4: Install SQL Server ToolsUpdate the sources list and install the unixODBC developer package:sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install mssql-tools unixodbc-devUpdate to the latest version of mssql-tools:sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install mssql-toolsAccept the license terms when prompted.Step 5: Update PATH Environment VariableAdd /opt/mssql-tools/bin/ to your PATH environment variable. For interactive sessions:echo 'export PATH="$PATH:/opt/mssql-tools/bin"' >> ~/.bash_profileFor non-interactive sessions:echo 'export PATH="$PATH:/opt/mssql-tools/bin"' >> ~/.bashrc source ~/.bashrcConnect to SQL Server LocallyConnect to your local SQL Server:sqlcmd -S localhost -U sa -P '<YourPassword>'Check the Installed SQL Server VersionCheck your current version and edition of SQL Server on Ubuntu:sqlcmd -S localhost -U SA -Q 'select @@VERSION'Create a SQL Server Database on UbuntuCreate a SQL Server database:CREATE DATABASE <database_name>; 
SELECT Name from sys.databases; 
GONote: The commands are not executed automatically. You need to enter GO from the new line to execute the previous commands.How to Connect Remotely to a SQL Server Running on UbuntuStep 1: Allow Remote Access to SQL ServerRun sqlcmd and execute the following query to allow remote access:EXEC sp_configure 'remote access', 0; GO RECONFIGURE; GOStep 2: Verify Remote ConnectivityFrom a remote machine, use sqlcmd or any SQL Server client tool to connect to the SQL Server instance. Replace <server-ip> with the IP address of your Ubuntu server and <YourPassword> with the SQL Server sa password.sqlcmd -S <server-ip> -U sa -P '<YourPassword>'If using a GUI tool like SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS), specify the server's IP address and port (e.g., <server-ip>,1433) along with the sa credentials., multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MysqlInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisSQL ManagerSQL ManagerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure Neo4JInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and configure Docker ContainerInstall and configure Docker ContainerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure MongoInstall and Configure MongoInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional) - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional) - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)More actionsInstall and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaSept 25, 20242 min readSnowflake Account Creation and Sign-Up Guide1. Account CreationsSign Up:Go to Snowflake Trial  and fill out the registration form.Login:Use the URL from the verification email to access the Snowflake Web Interface, then enter your email and password.2. SnowSQL Installation and Connection Guide1. Download and Install SnowSQLa. Download SnowSQLUse wget to download the SnowSQL installation script:wget https://sfc-repo.snowflakecomputing.com/snowsql/bootstrap/1.2/linux_x86_64/snowsql-1.2.31-linux_x86_64.bashb. Make the Installer ExecutableChange the permission of the downloaded file to make it executable:chmod +x snowsql-1.2.31-linux_x86_64.bashc. Run the InstallerExecute the installer script with sudo:sudo ./snowsql-1.2.31-linux_x86_64.bash3. Specify Installation DirectoryDuring the installation of SnowSQL, you will be prompted to specify the directory where SnowSQL components will be installed.a. Installation PromptWhen prompted with the following message:Specify the directory in which the SnowSQL components will be installed. [~/bin]b. Enter Installation DirectoryType in your desired installation path (e.g., /opt/snowsql) and press Enter:/opt/snowsql 4. Update PATH Variable and ConfirmAfter specifying the installation directory, you will be prompted:Do you want to add /opt/snowsql to PATH in /root/.profile? [y/N]Type y and press Enter to confirm. This updates the .profile file, allowing you to run SnowSQL from any terminal.Change Ownership and Permissions:sudo chown inferyx:inferyx /opt/snowsql 
sudo chmod +x /opt/snowsql5. Open a New Terminal SessionAfter updating the PATH variable, open a new terminal session to apply the changes.6. Test SnowSQL ConnectionTo verify your SnowSQL installation and connect to your Snowflake account, use the following command:snowsql -a <account_name> -u <username>Replace <account_name> with your Snowflake account name.Replace <username> with your Snowflake login username.7. Create SnowSQL Configuration FileCreate Configuration Directory:sudo mkdir ~/.snowsql 
sudo chmod +x ~/.snowsql 
sudo chown inferyx:inferyx ~/.snowsqlb. Create Configuration File:Edit the SnowSQL configuration file:sudo nano ~/.snowsql/configAdd your connection information to the ~/.snowsql/config file:[connections]
accountname = <account_name>
username = <login_name>
password = <password>After saving the file, secure it by setting the proper permissions:sudo chmod 600 ~/.snowsql/config
sudo chown inferyx:inferyx ~/.snowsql/config8. Create a Log FileTo create a log file for SnowSQL:sudo touch /snowsql_rt.log_bootstrap
sudo chown inferyx:inferyx /snowsql_rt.log_bootstrap9. Set Environment VariablesEdit your .bashrc file to add SnowSQL-specific environment variables:sudo nano ~/.bashrcAdd the following lines to the end of the file:export PATH=$PATH:/opt/snowsqlAfter editing, reload the .bashrc file to apply the changes:source ~/.bashrcSecure SnowSQL Directory:Ensure the SnowSQL directory has the correct ownership and permissions:sudo chown -R inferyx:inferyx /home/inferyx/.snowsql
sudo chmod 755 /home/inferyx/.snowsql10. Run SnowSQLAfter completing the setup, you can start SnowSQL by simply typing:snowsql11. SnowSQL InterfaceOnce logged in, you will see the following interface:SnowSQL * v1.2.32
Type SQL statements or !help
inferyx#COMPUTE_WH@(no database).(no schema)>            , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInferyx Project SetupInferyx Project SetupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MysqlInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall/update inferyx platformInstall/update inferyx platformInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisRelease Notes - 5.2.5.5Release Notes - 5.2.5.5Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Install and Configure Dremio (Optional) - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install and Configure Dremio (Optional) - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)More actionsInstall and Configure Dremio (Optional)Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaJul 22, 20241 min readPrerequisitesEnsure Java 11 is installed.Ensure wget is installed.Ensure tar is installed.User inferyx should exist.Steps to Install Confluent Platform OfflineStep 1: Download and Extract Dremio:wget https://download.dremio.com/community-server/dremio-community-LATEST.tar.gz
tar -xvf dremio-community-LATEST.tar.gz
sudo mv dremio-community-25.0.5-202407020141140611-dca7a083 /opt/dremio
sudo chown -R inferyx:inferyx /opt/dremioStep 2: Create Necessary Directories:sudo mkdir -p /var/lib/dremio /var/log/dremio /var/run/dremio
sudo chown -R inferyx:inferyx /var/lib/dremio /var/log/dremio /var/run/dremioStep 3: Configure System Limits:Edit /etc/security/limits.conf:sudo nano /etc/security/limits.confAdd the following lines at the end:inferyx soft nofile 65536
inferyx hard nofile 65536Edit /etc/pam.d/common-session:sudo nano /etc/pam.d/common-sessionAdd the following line:session required pam_limits.soStep 4: Create Systemd Service:Create and edit /etc/systemd/system/dremio.service:sudo nano /etc/systemd/system/dremio.serviceAdd the following content:[Unit]
Description=Dremio
After=network.target
[Service]
Type=forking
User=inferyx
ExecStart=/opt/dremio/bin/dremio start
ExecStop=/opt/dremio/bin/dremio stop
LimitNOFILE=65536
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
Set permissions:sudo chown -R inferyx:inferyx /etc/systemd/system/dremio.serviceStep 5: Set Java Home in Dremio Script:Edit /opt/dremio/bin/dremio:sudo nano /opt/dremio/bin/dremioAdd the following line at the beginning:export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-11-openjdk-amd64Step 6: Start and Enable Dremio Service:sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable dremio
sudo systemctl start dremio
sudo systemctl status dremioStep 7: Verification:To verify Dremio is running correctly, check the service status:sudo systemctl status dremioEnsure Dremio is accessible from the web interface at http://<your-server-ip>:9047.NotesIf there are any issues with file permissions or user configurations, verify the ownership and permissions of the directories and files as mentioned in the steps.Ensure that the JAVA_HOME path is correctly set to the Java 11 installation directory.This guide provides a comprehensive overview of setting up Dremio on an Ubuntu system. Adjust the paths and configurations as necessary for your specific environment., multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall and Configure Genai (Optional)Install and Configure Genai (Optional)Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure CamundaInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInferyx Project SetupInferyx Project SetupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall/update inferyx platformInstall/update inferyx platformInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure Neo4JInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Install and Configure Kafka-Confluent 7.8.0 - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install and Configure Kafka-Confluent 7.8.0 - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka-Confluent 7.8.0Install and Configure Kafka-Confluent 6.1.1Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install and Configure Kafka-Confluent 7.8.0More actionsInstall and Configure Kafka-Confluent 7.8.0Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Apr 30, 20251 min read1. Download and Install Confluent PlatformStep 1: Download the Confluent Platform Tarballcurl -O https://packages.confluent.io/archive/7.8/confluent-community-7.8.0.tar.gzStep 2: Extract the Tarballtar -xvf confluent-community-7.8.0.tar.gzStep 3: Move the Extracted Files to /opt/sudo mv confluent-7.8.0/ /opt/Step 4: Change Directory to Confluent Installation Foldercd /opt/confluent-7.8.0/Step 5: Verify the Confluent Platform Filesls /opt/confluent-7.8.0 2. Set Environment VariablesStep 1: Set the CONFLUENT_HOME Environment Variableexport CONFLUENT_HOME=/opt/confluent-7.8.0Step 2: Add Confluent Platform Binaries to the PATHexport PATH=$PATH:$CONFLUENT_HOME/binStep 3: Verify the Environment Variablesecho $CONFLUENT_HOME
echo $PATH3. Install and Configure Confluent Hub ClientStep 1: Create a Temporary Directorymkdir -p /tmp/kafkaStep 2: Download the Confluent Hub Clientwget http://client.hub.confluent.io/confluent-hub-client-latest.tar.gzStep 3: Extract the Confluent Hub Clienttar -xvf confluent-hub-client-latest.tar.gzStep 4: Copy Files to the Confluent Platform Directorycp -R bin/* /opt/confluent-7.8.0/bin/
cp -R etc/* /opt/confluent-7.8.0/etc/
cp -R share/java/* /opt/confluent-7.8.0/share/java/4. Install Kafka Connector for MongoDBStep 1: Install the MongoDB Kafka ConnectorUse the Confluent Hub Client to install the MongoDB Kafka Connector version 1.10.0:confluent-hub install mongodb/kafka-connect-mongodb:1.10.05. Install Confluent CLIStep 1: Create the APT Keyrings Directorysudo mkdir -p /etc/apt/keyringsStep 2: Download and Add the Confluent CLI GPG Keycurl https://packages.confluent.io/confluent-cli/deb/archive.key | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/confluent-cli.gpgStep 3: Set Permissions on the GPG Keysudo chmod go+r /etc/apt/keyrings/confluent-cli.gpgStep 4: Add the Confluent CLI Repository to APT Sourcesecho "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/confluent-cli.gpg] https://packages.confluent.io/confluent-cli/deb stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/confluent-cli.list >/dev/nullStep 5: Update the APT Package Listsudo apt updateStep 6: Install Confluent CLIsudo apt install confluent-cli6. Verify Confluent Platform InstallationStep 1: Check Confluent CLI Versionconfluent versionStep 2: Verify Kafka Broker and Zookeeperconfluent local services status7. Start and Stop Confluent ServicesStep 1: Start Servicesconfluent local services startStep 2: Check Services Statusconfluent local services statusStep 3: Stop Servicesconfluent local services stop, multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall and Configure Kafka-Confluent 6.1.1Install and Configure Kafka-Confluent 6.1.1Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure Neo4JInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and configure Docker ContainerInstall and configure Docker ContainerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisHubHubInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

The example provided uses version 6.1.1 of the Confluent Platform.PrerequisitesLinux-based systemInternet access to download the Confluent Platform package (optional if you have the package locally)Steps to Install Confluent Platform OfflineStep 1: Download and Extract Confluent PlatformNavigate to the desired directory, e.g., /app.cd /install/Download the Confluent Platform tarball. If you have internet access:sudo wget http://packages.confluent.io/archive/6.1/confluent-6.1.1.tar.gzIf you don't have internet access, ensure you have the confluent-6.1.1.tar.gz file available locally and skip to the next step.Extract the tarball.sudo tar -xvf confluent-6.1.1.tar.gzMove the extracted directory to /opt.sudo mv confluent-6.1.1 /optStep 2: Set Environment VariablesSet the CONFLUENT_HOME environment variable to the Confluent Platform directory.export CONFLUENT_HOME=/opt/confluent-6.1.1Add the Confluent Platform bin directory to your PATH.export PATH=$PATH:$CONFLUENT_HOME/bin(Optional) To make these changes permanent, add the above lines to your shell profile (e.g., .bashrc or .profile).echo 'export CONFLUENT_HOME=/opt/confluent-6.1.1' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export PATH=$PATH:$CONFLUENT_HOME/bin' >> ~/.bashrc source ~/.bashrcStep 3: Start Confluent ServicesStart the Confluent services locally.confluent local services startStep 4: Install Kafka Connect Plugins(optional)Navigate to the desired directory, e.g., /opt/confluent-6.1.1/bin/cd /opt/confluent-6.1.1/bin/Install the Kafka Connect JDBC plugin../confluent-hub install confluentinc/kafka-connect-jdbc:latestInstall the Kafka Connect Neo4j plugin../confluent-hub install neo4j/kafka-connect-neo4j:2.0.1Step 5: Access Confluent Control CenterOpen your browser and navigate to http://your_ip_addres:9021 to access the Confluent Control Center.SummaryThis document provides the necessary steps to install Confluent Platform offline and configure the environment to start using Kafka and its associated services. Follow the steps carefully to ensure a smooth installation and setup. If you encounter any issues, refer to the Confluent Platform documentation or community forums for assistance., multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall and Configure Kafka-Confluent 7.8.0Install and Configure Kafka-Confluent 7.8.0Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure Neo4JInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisInstall and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Project  Deployment Process - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Project  Deployment Process - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideProject  Deployment ProcessPatch Deployment ProcessDocker Deployment ProcessProject White-lable ProcessAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Project  Deployment ProcessMore actionsProject  Deployment ProcessYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Feb 04, 20251 min readRequest the Download FileChoose one of the below options to download the patch file.Option 1: Using curlTo obtain the necessary download file for the patch, use the following curl command. This will authenticate and download the required tar file directly to your system.curl https://dev.inferyx.com/download/inferyx-platform-final-v5.4.4.5.tar.gzFile Name: inferyx-platform-final-v5.4.4.5.tar.gzOROption 2: Using scpAlternatively, you can securely copy (scp) the tar file from the remote server. Replace <username>, <remote_host>, and /path/to/ with the appropriate values.scp <username>@<remote_host>:/path/to/inferyx-platform-final-v5.4.4.5.tar.gz .Note: The dot (.) at the end of the command saves the file to your current working directory.Once you have the tar file, extract it into the /install/framework/build/ directory:tar -xvf /install/inferyx-platform-final-v5.4.4.5.tar.gz   3.Run the deploy_framework.sh ScriptNavigate cd /install/inferyx-platform-final-v5.4.4.5/deploy/bin to the  Execute the install_framework.sh script with the required options do:./install_framework.sh -h./install_framework.sh -o all -a all -d framework -u inferyx -p 20Inferyx19Run the create_all_db.sh ScriptNavigate to the cd /app/framework/bin/ directory and run the create_all_db.sh script with the required parameters:./create_all_db.sh -o common,inferyx -a all -s all -u inferyx -p inferyx -t mysqlLicense File Request the license files from the Inferyx team and place them in the appropriate directory as specified by the documentation or instructions provided by the Inferyx team.Run the deploy.sh script Navigate to the cd /install/framework/build/inferyx-platform-final-v5.4.4.5/deploy/bin directory in the framework build and execute the upgrade_framework.sh script:./upgrade_framework.sh -u inferyx -p 20Inferyx19 -b no -r noThe -r option is not mandatory. Use this option only if you need to patch the same version again.7. Access the Platform Open web browser and verify that the application is accessible and you can log in as a user:URL: http://localhost:8080/framework/app/index.html, multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapsePatch Deployment ProcessPatch Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDocker Deployment ProcessDocker Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInferyx Project SetupInferyx Project SetupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisProject White-lable ProcessProject White-lable ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Docker Deployment Process - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Docker Deployment Process - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideProject  Deployment ProcessPatch Deployment ProcessDocker Deployment ProcessProject White-lable ProcessAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Docker Deployment ProcessMore actionsDocker Deployment ProcessYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Sept 16, 20241 min readPrerequisitesDocker installed on the host machine.Docker credentials provided by the Inferyx team (Token, Username, Docker Image).1. Login to DockerAuthenticate your Docker client with the provided credentials.docker login -u inferyxhub2. Pull the Inferyx Docker ImageRetrieve the latest version of the Inferyx platform Docker image from the registry.docker pull inferyxhub/inferyx-platform:latest3. Run the Docker ContainerStart the Inferyx platform by running the Docker container. Choose one of the following methods based on your network and port mapping requirements:Option 1: Using Host NetworkingThis option maps the container directly to the host's network. Adjust volume mappings as needed.docker run --network host -d --name inferyx-platform \ -v app:/app/framework \ -v jvm:/usr/lib/jvm \ -v mysqld:/var/run/mysqld \ -v log:/var/log/inferyx \ inferyxhub/inferyx-platform:latestOption 2: Using Port MappingThis option maps specific ports from the container to the host. Adjust ports if necessary.docker run -d --name inferyx-platform \ -p 8081:8080 \ -p 27018:27017 \ -p 3307:3306 \ -p 9091:9090 \ -p 7475:7474 \ -p 7688:7687 \ inferyxhub/inferyx-platform:latest4. Verify Platform InitializationAllow approximately 5 minutes for the container to initialize and the Inferyx platform to start up fully.To verify the deployment, open a web browser and navigate to:http://localhost:8080/framework/app/index.htmlIf the platform is running correctly, you should see the Inferyx application interface. , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall and configure Docker ContainerInstall and configure Docker ContainerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisProject  Deployment ProcessProject  Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisPatch Deployment ProcessPatch Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure MongoInstall and Configure MongoInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisInstall and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisAtlassian Intelligence

5.x/Project White-lable ProcessMore actionsProject White-lable ProcessYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaSept 16, 20241 min read Navigate to the Deployment Directory First, change to the deployment directory where the update_config.sh script is located:cd /app/framework/bin/View Available Options To view the available options for the update_config.sh script, run:./update_config.sh -hThis will display the help message and list of options.Options:-c, --company:            Company name (e.g., Nam Info).-U, --username:          MongoDB username (e.g., inferyx).-P, --password:          MongoDB password (e.g., 20Inferyx19).-o, --org:            Organization name (e.g., Nam Info).-lb, --logo-black:    Logo black name with .png extension (e.g., /backup/img/logo.png).-l, --link:                 Website link (e.g., https://nam-it.com)Run the update_config.sh script Execute the update_config.sh script with the required parameters. ./update_config.sh -c 'Inferyx Inc' -U inferyx -P 20Inferyx19 -o 'Inferyx Inc' -lb /backup/img/logo.png -l 'https://inferyx.com'Verify Configuration After running the script, ensure that the configuration has been updated correctly. You can check the application’s configuration or logs to confirm the changes have been applied.  , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseProject  Deployment ProcessProject  Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInferyx Project SetupInferyx Project SetupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisStepsStepsInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisPatch Deployment ProcessPatch Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDocker Deployment ProcessDocker Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsOKTA AuthenticationOKTA Redirect Model Using SAML 2.0OKTA Embedded Model using API AuthenticationLDAP AuthenticationInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/OKTA AuthenticationMore actionsOKTA AuthenticationYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Jul 15, 20222 min readOKTA Server Configurations :Please follow the following steps below for OKTA server setup.Use the URL https://login.okta.com/signin/  to register for an account on the OKTA portal. Following registration, you will receive a verification email at the address you provided. Once verified, sign in to your OKTA account.  Open  Ones logging in, you have option either purchase a server instance or use the free trial for a month. If you're using the free version, select the trial option, enter all the required info as shown below, and then select "Get started."Open After submission you will get domain Org URL for OKTA server as shown below ex. https://trial-2786921.okta.com. Sign in to your OKTA account using Org URL.Open  Open admin console , there you will get all option available like add users, permissions , security etc.Open  Select "People" under "Directory" to get a list of users who have been added by application. If you want to add a new user, click the "Add person" button and provide all the necessary information as shown in the screenshot below.Open   We have implemented both model Redirect and Embedded Authentication.OKTA Redirect Model Using SAML 2.0OKTA Embedded Model using  API Authentication , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseOKTA Embedded Model using API AuthenticationOKTA Embedded Model using API AuthenticationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisOKTA Redirect Model Using SAML 2.0OKTA Redirect Model Using SAML 2.0Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAccountAccountInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisApplicationApplicationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisUserUserInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisOrganizationOrganizationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

Navigate to Applications, click  “Create App Integration” and choose “SAML 2.0” as shown below snap.Open  Give your name to the application and then select next.Open  Enter the single sign-on as http://localhost:8080/security/okta/validate for Inferyx application redirection URL, then click next and finishOpen  After creating an application successfully, Navigate on the "Sign on" tab and then click the "View SAML Setup Instructions" button as shown in the image below.Open   Download and Save the certificate file in the project location: /app/framework/saml/Okta.cert for verification.Open  Navigate to “Assignment” tab for Assigning Users to Application  as shown in below image.Open  OKTA Configuration in Inferyx application : Configure the OKTA Certification path , logoutUrl in framework.properties file. The properties are defined as below.framework.auth.okta.cert=/app/framework/saml/okta2.certframework.auth.okta.logoutUrl=https://trial-2786921.okta.com/app/UserHome Note : Inferyx's mongodb database must have the OKTA users in handling authorization., multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseOKTA Embedded Model using API AuthenticationOKTA Embedded Model using API AuthenticationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisOKTA AuthenticationOKTA AuthenticationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisLDAP AuthenticationLDAP AuthenticationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure CamundaInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisHow to create a organizationHow to create a organizationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisApplicationApplicationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Installation and Configure Studio 3T - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:

SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideTech StackInstallation ChecklistInferyx Project SetupInstall and Configure Java 8 ,11,17,21Install and Configure Tomcat 9.0Install and Configure Tomcat 10Install and Configure MysqlInstall and Configure MongoInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall and Configure Neo4JInstall and Configure CamundaInstall and Configure Kubeflow 1.26.0Install and Configure JupyterHub 3.1.1Install and Configure Python (Optional)Install and Configure Mssql (Optional)Install and Configure Snowflake (Optional)Install and Configure Dremio (Optional)Install and Configure Kafka (Optional)Project Setup  and Docker Deployment GuideAuthentication ConfigurationsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Installation and Configure Studio 3TMore actionsInstallation and Configure Studio 3TYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Jan 31, 20251 min readDownload the Studio 3T InstallerFirst, download the Studio 3T installer for Linux from the official download page.Extract the Tar FileOnce the tar.gz file is downloaded, follow these steps to extract it:Open the terminal.Navigate to the folder where the tar.gz file is downloaded:cd ~/DownloadsExtract the file using the following command:tar -xvzf studio-3t-linux-x64.tar.gz This will create a directory named studio-3t-linux-x64 containing the extracted files.Make the installation script executable (if it's not already):chmod +x studio-3t-linux-x64.sh Run the Installer./studio-3t-linux-x64.shThe script will begin by unpacking the required Java Runtime Environment (JRE) for Studio 3T to function correctly.Follow the Installation StepsThe installer will open a graphical user interface (GUI) to guide you through the installation:Choose the installation path: You can either accept the default installation path or specify a custom directory.Accept the End-User License Agreement (EULA): Read and accept the terms of the EULA.Proceed with installation: Click the "Install" button to start the installation process.Alternatively, a desktop shortcut may have been created during the installation process, which you can use to launch Studio 3T.First-Time SetupUpon launching Studio 3T for the first time, you’ll be prompted to connect to a MongoDB instance:Enter the necessary connection details for your MongoDB server (e.g., hostname, port, authentication credentials).After successfully connecting, you’ll be able to view your MongoDB databases and collections in the left sidebar of the Studio 3T interface., multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall/update inferyx platformInstall/update inferyx platformInferyx Platform Documentation - main_content:
User Login - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileLock ScreenAbout UsThemeUser LoginLog OutGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/User LoginMore actionsUser LoginYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: May 14, 20241 min readIntroductionWelcome to the documentation outlining the user interface changes for the user login application module based on application type. This document presents the varying permissions and functionalities accessible within the system based on different application types: standard application, administrative application, and system administration application.The purpose of this documentation is to provide detailed guidance on the distinct user experiences and menu configurations tailored to each application type upon logging into the system. By understanding the specific permissions and menu structures corresponding to each application category, administrators and developers can effectively manage user access and system administration.Application Type PermissionsThis documentation will cover the following Application Type  permissions and corresponding menu changes:1. Standard Application Permissions:Upon logging into the standard application, users will have access to specific menu options under the admin menu designed for standard application functionalities.Activity CalendarDatastoreDomainMetadataExport ImportApplication SettingsFile ManagerPredict Server2. Account Admin Application Logging into the administrative application grants access to additional administrative features under the admin menu.Activity Application Calendar Datasource Datastore Domain title: Dashboard - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Dashboard - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationDashboardVizpodCubepodReportDV Parameter ListDV ResultsArchivesData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/DashboardMore actionsDashboardYogesh PalrechaPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Shreejit Deo (Unlicensed)Owned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Oct 28, 20244 min readIntroductionA dashboard is a powerful interface designed to provide users with a consolidated and visually intuitive overview of various data points. It allows users to access and analyze multiple charts seamlessly integrated within a single screen, offering a holistic perspective on key metrics and insights. Each chart is attentively configured as a Vizpod within the Inferyx platform, ensuring not only a streamlined and efficient visualization experience but also enabling users to make informed decisions based on comprehensive data representations. Dashboards play a crucial role in facilitating data-driven decision-making by presenting complex information in a user-friendly format, enhancing accessibility, and promoting efficiency across professional settings.Create Dashboard1. Click Data Visualization > Click Dashboard  Open  2. Click Create Dashboard Open  3. Click Continue Upon completing all required details, click the "Continue" button to advance to the subsequent configuration steps for the dashboard.Open   Properties :Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault UUIDUnique identifier of the Dashboard.  NameName of the Dashboard.Y Display NameDisplay the name of the Dashboard.Y VersionThe version number for the Dashboard. This is an audit column.   Created ByThe user who created the Dashboard. This is an audit column.  Created OnDate and Time of Creation.  TagsTags can be used for searching metadata.N DescDescription of the Dashboard.N FilterSelect fields to be applied in dashboard filtersDashboard level filter which applies to all pages.  ActiveActivate / De-Activate the Dashboard.YYLockedRestricts any modifications to the Dashboard. It can only be unlocked by an application admin user.YNPublishedIt makes the rule visible to other users within the same application.YYPublicIt makes the Dashboard visible in read-only mode to all users across the platform.YNScopeDashboard Scope Organization/Global/Application  TemplateList Of Notification Template  TypeDashboard SUMMARY/DETAIL. NRefresh On OpenRefresh dashboards while opening the dashboard.  4.FilterTo Add Filter Click --> Add Open Click --> Submit Filter Properties Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault IDUnique identifier of the Filter  TypeSelect The Type Datapod /Dataset  NameName Of The Selected Datapod/Dataset  AttributeName Of Attribute For Filter  Display NameDisplay Name For The Filter  Filter Type Type Of Filter  MandatorySelect For Mandatory Filter  Default TypeType Of Filter Function/String/Number  Default ValueValue For The Filter FIRST/LAST/MIN/MAX  Sort OrderSorting Filter Ascending/Descending  Apply DistinctSelect For Distinct Value In Filter  Parent FilterLevel Of The Filters    4.To add privileges for dashboards Click -->  Privilege Icon On Right Corner1.User Level Privilege 2.UserGroup Level Privilege Open Click --> Grant --> Add User for Privilege --> SubmitOpen  For Revoke Privilege --> click -->Revoke Access5.HelpThe 'Help' feature provides quick access to Inferyx documentation. By clicking the help icon, users are instantly navigated to relevant resources for support and guidance.Click --> Help IconOpen  6. Adding Vizpod (Chart) to the dashboardTo enhance the dashboard, simply add Vizpods one by one and leverage of its ability to display one or more charts for visualization.Select Vizpod >Edit the title for chart > Click SubmitOpen 7. Adding Multiple charts on a single page Navigate to the edit page and click the '+' icon. Select the desired Vizpod from the drop-down menu. Utilize the drag-and-drop functionality to adjust the layout as needed. Finally, Submit the changes for implementation.Open  8.  Expanding Dashboard Functionality with Multiple Tabs and Tab-Level Filters Enhance the dashboard by clicking on +Page to incorporate a new tab.Configure tab-level filters effortlessly by selecting the Filter icon.Filters can be applied at either the dashboard level, affecting all pages, or at the page level. Dashboard-level filters have a global impact, influencing all pages within the dashboard, and can be configured while creating the dashboards. Additionally, filter values can be leveraged to parameterize custom queries, enhancing the flexibility of creating and using custom datasets to create Vizpods by seamlessly replacing placeholders.Open 9. Double-click on cards to view the DashboardOpen  Open 10.Click --> LensOpen   Add Lens --> SubmitOpen 11. Click > Download PDF icon on the right side cornerOpen 12. Click > Download icon to download records from individual VizpodOpen  13. Click --> Drill To Detail icon to Display All Rerecords from individual VizpodOpen 14.Click --> Show Sql To Sisplay SQL Query Of Individual VizpodOpen  , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseAnalytics WorkbenchAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisNotebook ManagerNotebook ManagerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData ManagementData ManagementInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisActivityActivityInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisModelModelInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisReportReportInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Vizpod - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Vizpod - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationDashboardVizpodCubepodReportDV Parameter ListDV ResultsArchivesData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/VizpodMore actionsVizpodYogesh PalrechaPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Shreejit Deo (Unlicensed)Owned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Sept 30, 20243 min readIntroductionA Vizpod represents a singular chart within the dashboard, allowing users to visualize specific data. Users have the flexibility to choose the Source Data, Chart Type, Key, and Values, as well as make cosmetic adjustments. This customization empowers users to effectively visualize and interpret data within the dashboard. Create VizpodClick Data Visualization > Click VizpodOpen 2. Click Add  Open 3. Configuring Vizpod    a) Fill in necessary fields > Click SubmitOpen  b) To create a Network Graph A network graph is a visual representation of connections between different entities. It consists of nodes (representing entities) and edges (representing relationships or connections between entities). In simpler terms, it helps to illustrate how various things are related to each other in a structured way, making complex relationships easier to understand at a glance.The Inferyx platform offers versatile visualization capabilities for network graphs. In this context, the source type is represented by Graphpods, which are knowledge graphs connected to the Neo4j database.Creating a Network Graph involves utilizing a dedicated panel where users can input Cypher queries, allowing for dynamic and tailored exploration of the data. Cypher queries can be parameterized using placeholders, allowing their values to be replaced by filter values. This flexibility enhances the adaptability of the queries, enabling users to refine and tailor their exploration based on specific criteria or conditions.Open   Vizpod Definition:Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault UUIDUnique identifier of the Vizpod.  NameName of the Vizpod.Y Display NameDisplay the name of the Vizpod.Y VersionThe version number for the Vizpod. This is an audit column.   Created ByThe user who created the Vizpod. This is an audit column.  Created OnDate and Time of Creation.  TagsTags can be used for searching metadata.N DescDescription of the Vizpod.N SourceType of source.Y NameName of source.Y TypeType of chart.Y Title Font ColorColor of the title font.  Title Font SizeSize of the title font.  Show Column TotalShows column totals (applicable for grid views).  Show Row TotalShows row totals (applicable for grid views).  Color PaletteA color palette is the full range of colors that can be displayed for vizpod.  LimitTo specify the number of records to return. -1Sort BySorting attribute.  Sort OrderTypes of sorting, DESC/ASC.  Detail AttributeSelect the list of attributes.  FootnoteAn additional piece of information printed at the bottom of a page.  ActiveActivate / De-Activate the rule.YYLockedRestricts any modifications to the Vizpod. Can only be unlocked by the application admin user.YNPublishedIt makes the Vizpod visible to other users within the same application.YYPublicIt makes the Vizpod visible in read-only mode to all users across the platform.YNDrill-to-Detailselect the name of attributes to Display Data  Attributeselect the name of attributes to be used.  KeySelect key attribute .Y GroupGroups rows that have the same values.N ValueSelect the attribute which contains the values.Y QueryPanel to write cypher queries only for network-graph.  LegendsA Legend is a representation of legend keys or entries on the plotted area of a chart or graph.  StatusCheck the validity of meta.  TemplateList Of Notification Template  Additional features Sample Records    This is the  sample of data used to create vizpod Click the Sample Records  iconOpen  Show SQLClick the Show SQL iconOpen  Click > Action > ViewTo view all configurations of vizpod.Open  , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseCubepodCubepodInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisDatapodDatapodInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisModelModelInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisEntityEntityInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisActivityActivityInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisReportReportInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

Data Management - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementData IngestionData QualityData ProfilingData PreparationData ReconciliationData ArchivalData MigrationData VaultData PipelineBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Data ManagementMore actionsData ManagementYogesh PalrechaPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Kiran P (Unlicensed)Owned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Dec 19, 20231 min readIntroductionData Management package provides an end-to-end data management capabilities to extract, transform and load data between multiple systems. There are various modules that are available under Data Management. Data Ingestion module is used to extract data in various formats like files, tables etc. and load into a target data repository like Data Warehouse, Data Vault or a Data Lake. Transformations on data can be performed using Data Preparation module. It also provides capabilities to configure Data Quality, Data Profiling and Data Reconciliation rules on the underlying data. These modules can be executed independently or combined into a Data Pipeline and executed together. Retention and Archival policies can be configured using Data Archival module.Modules :Data IngestionData QualityData ProfilingData PreparationData ReconciliationData ArchivalData VaultData Pipeline    , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseData ArchivalData ArchivalInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData PipelineData PipelineInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData MigrationData MigrationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData QualityData QualityInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData VisualizationData VisualizationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisData IngestionData IngestionInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementData IngestionIngest RuleIngest Rule GroupIngest ResultsIngestion Parameter ListData QualityData ProfilingData PreparationData ReconciliationData ArchivalData MigrationData VaultData PipelineBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Data IngestionMore actionsData IngestionYogesh PalrechaPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Kiran P (Unlicensed)Shreejit Deo (Unlicensed)Owned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Dec 19, 20232 min readIntroductionData ingestion is an operational process that involves the seamless transfer of data from multiple source points to a designated destination for subsequent processing and analysis. This encompassing procedure encompasses diverse scenarios, ranging from loading data originating in files to tables, inter-table transfers, streaming data integration into tables, and more. It supports a versatile framework that accommodates both real-time and batch-oriented data movement, offering the flexibility of both pull and push mechanisms. The overarching goal of data ingestion is to facilitate efficient and timely data acquisition, laying the foundation for comprehensive analytics and further downstream processing. Collection To Table - Load data from the Document into a Table File To File - Load data from files(CSV, Excel) into files(CSV, Excel)  File To FTP - Load data from files(CSV, Excel) into an FTP file File To Graph - Load data from files(CSV, Excel) into the Graph databaseFile To Table - Load data from files(CSV, Excel) into a Table FTP To File - Load data from FTP files into files(CSV, Excel) FTP To Table - Load data from FTP files into a TableGraph To File - Load data from FTP files into files (CSV, Excel)Graph To Table - Load data from the Graph database into a TableRest To File - Load data from API Response into files(CSV, Excel)Rest To Table - Load data from API Response into a TableStream To File - Load data from Kafka streams into  files (CSV, Excel)Stream To Table - Load data from Kafka streams into a Table Table To File -  Load data from the table into  files (CSV, Excel)Table To FTP -  Load data from the table into the FTP fileTable To Graph -  Load data from the table into the Graph databaseTable To Stream -  Load data from the table into the Kafka streamsTable To Table - Load data from the table into a table Web Service To File - Load data from Web Service into files(CSV, Excel)Web Service To Table - Load data from Web Service into a TableTypes of Data Ingestion RulesReal-time data ingestionReal-time data ingestion is the process of collecting and transferring data from source systems in real-time using solutions such as change data capture (CDC). CDC constantly monitors transactions or redo logs and moves changed data without interfering with the database workload. Real-time ingestion is essential for time-sensitive use cases, such as stock market trading or power grid monitoring, when organizations have to rapidly react to new information. Real-time data pipelines are also vital when making rapid operational decisions and identifying and acting on new insights.Batch-based data ingestionBatch-based data ingestion is the process of collecting and transferring data in batches according to scheduled intervals. The ingestion layer may collect data based on simple schedules, trigger events, or any other logical ordering. Batch-based ingestion is useful when companies need to collect specific data points on a daily basis or simply don’t need data for real-time decision-making. Ingest RuleIngest Rule GroupIngest ResultIngestion Parameter, multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseIngest RuleIngest RuleInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisIngest Rule GroupIngest Rule GroupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDatasetDatasetInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDatapodDatapodInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisBatchBatchInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisAnalytics WorkbenchAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

Data Quality - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementData IngestionData QualityDQ RuleDQ Rule GroupDQ ResultsDQ Parameter ListRecommenderBatch CreateCompare ResultData ProfilingData PreparationData ReconciliationData ArchivalData MigrationData VaultData PipelineBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Data QualityMore actionsData QualityYogesh PalrechaPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Kiran P (Unlicensed)Shreejit Deo (Unlicensed)Owned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Dec 19, 20231 min readIntroductionData quality is an evaluative metric gauging the state of data, considering key factors such as accuracy, completeness, consistency, reliability, and currency. This multifaceted assessment is integral for organizations seeking to discern potential data discrepancies requiring rectification and to ascertain the fitness of data within their IT systems for its intended operational purposes.The Data Quality Module is specifically engineered to conduct meticulous evaluations, both at the attribute and table levels. Attribute-level checks encompass critical assessments such as Duplicate Checks and Null Check, with provision for customized quality checks tailored to specific business requirements. Concurrently, table-level data quality evaluations are conducted, focusing on critical aspects like Duplicate Checks and Referential Integrity Checks. This comprehensive approach ensures a thorough examination of data quality, enabling organizations to maintain the integrity and effectiveness of their data assets.Data Quality checks can be applied only on Datapod. The data quality rule establishes standards for evaluating the condition of data based on various parameters. These parameters typically include factors such as accuracy, completeness, consistency, reliability, and timeliness.Within the context of a data quality framework, a data quality rule is essential for ensuring that the data meets predefined standards and aligns with the organization's data quality objectives. These rules can be applied at different levels, including attribute-level checks, where individual data attributes are examined for adherence to specified criteria (e.g., checking for duplicates or ensuring non-null values), and table-level checks that assess overall data quality, such as examining data for duplicates or ensuring referential integrity.1. The execution of the rules contained within the group can be carried out through two distinct methodologies: the first being parallel execution, where rules operate concurrently, and the second involves executing the rules collectively as a cohesive group. This flexible operational framework allows for tailored approaches, enabling organizations to optimize the execution strategy based on specific requirements and performance considerations.1. Data Profiling - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementData IngestionData QualityData ProfilingDP RuleDP Rule GroupDP ResultsDP Compare ResultsDP Parameter ListData PreparationData ReconciliationData ArchivalData MigrationData VaultData PipelineBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Data ProfilingMore actionsData ProfilingYogesh PalrechaSukalyan Hazari (Unlicensed)Pavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Shreejit Deo (Unlicensed)Owned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Dec 19, 20231 min readIntroductionData Profiling represents a systematic procedure for scrutinizing the information derived from an extant information source, such as Datapod, with the aim of gathering comprehensive statistical insights and informative summaries pertaining to the dataset. This process stands as a critical tool for organizations seeking to enhance their decision-making processes through a more informed understanding of their data. The regular execution of data profiling enables the generation of alerts based on variations in results, facilitating proactive management of data quality. Conventionally, data profiling is conducted subsequent to the data loading phase into tables, typically following Data Ingestion or Mapping processes. This strategic approach ensures a robust assessment of data attributes and characteristics for subsequent analytical and decision-making endeavors. Data Profiling is only applicable for Datapods.Data Profiling produces the following results on attributes.Number of RowsMin, Max, Average and Standard DeviationNumber and % of Distinct ValuesNumber and % of Null ValuesMin, Max and Average LengthNumber of Duplicates DP RuleDP Rule GroupDP ResultsDP Compare  ResultsDP Parameter List, multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseDatasetDatasetInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData ManagementData ManagementInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisData PreparationData PreparationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisData QualityData QualityInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisData ReconciliationData ReconciliationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisAnalytics WorkbenchAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

DP Rule - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementData IngestionData QualityData ProfilingDP RuleDP Rule GroupDP ResultsDP Compare ResultsDP Parameter ListData PreparationData ReconciliationData ArchivalData MigrationData VaultData PipelineBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/DP RuleMore actionsDP RuleSukalyan Hazari (Unlicensed)Yogesh PalrechaPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Shreejit Deo (Unlicensed)Owned by Sukalyan Hazari (Unlicensed)Last updated: Sept 26, 2024 by Yogesh Palrecha3 min readA Data Profile Rule is a predefined criterion or set of criteria employed to assess and analyze specific characteristics of a dataset, typically aimed at gaining insights into its structure, quality, and content. These rules are systematically applied to the dataset to generate a comprehensive data profile, providing valuable information about various attributes, such as data distribution, uniqueness, completeness, and statistical summaries.Data Profile Rules serve as a powerful tool in data management, enabling organizations to understand the nature and quality of their data. These rules can encompass a range of analyses, including but not limited to frequency distributions, pattern matching, uniqueness checks, and statistical computations. The results derived from the application of Data Profile Rules contribute to data governance efforts, aiding in the identification of data anomalies, outliers, and potential areas for improvement.In essence, Data Profile Rules play a crucial role in the data profiling process, facilitating the exploration and understanding of dataset characteristics, which, in turn, supports data-driven decision-making and enhances overall data quality.1. This grouping facilitates the streamlined execution of multiple Data Profile rules, allowing for both sequential and parallel processing. The organized orchestration of these rules within a designated rule group enhances operational efficiency and provides a systematic approach to data profiling. The flexibility to execute multiple Data Profile Rules in a tailored sequence or concurrently within a rule group exemplifies a robust framework for comprehensive data profiling initiatives. This structured approach supports organizations in systematically assessing and understanding their data, contributing to enhanced data quality and informed decision-making.1. SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementData IngestionData QualityData ProfilingData PreparationDatapodDatasetExpressionFormulaFunctionMapRelationParameter ListDM OperatorData ReconciliationData ArchivalData MigrationData VaultData PipelineBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/DatapodMore actionsDatapodKiran P (Unlicensed)Yogesh PalrechaPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Shreejit Deo (Unlicensed)Owned by Kiran P (Unlicensed)Last updated: Oct 28, 2024 by Yogesh Palrecha6 min readDatapod serves as a database table reference within the Inferyx Platform, with its associated metadata stored in MongoDB. This comprehensive integration allows users to not only create intricate views within the database but also seamlessly register these complex views as datapods on the Inferyx Platform. By enabling users to harness the flexibility of creating and registering complex views, Inferyx facilitates a sophisticated approach to data modeling and analysis. This integration underscores the platform's commitment to accommodating diverse data structures and empowering users to derive valuable insights from their data with efficiency and precision.FeaturesList Datapod Create DatapodShow Meta ComparePreview Show Data StoreData ProfilingData QualityData LineageShow SQLShow Dependencies DownloadUpload PrivilageHelp 1. List Datapod Click on Data Preparation  -->  Datapod Open 2. Create Datapod Click Add Open  3. Configure Datapoda) Fill Necessary field > Click SubmitEnter Datapod DetailsUnder Attribute Detail > EditEnter attributes informationClick > SubmitNOTE: In datapod, at least one primary key should be there which is for the index column.Open  Open PropertiesField NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault UUIDUnique identifier of the datapod  NameName of the datapodY Display NameDisplay the name of the datapodY VersionThe version number for the datapod. This is an audit column.   CreatedByThe user who created the datapod. This is an audit column.  TagsAllows tagging of the meta. Tags can be used for searching metadata.N DescDescription of the datapodN Datapod TypeType of the datapod, eg. TABLE,FILE,VIEW.Y CategoryCategory of That Datapod NData SourceType of the source. eg. MYSQL etc.Y NameName of the data sourceY ActiveActivate / De-Activate the ruleYYLockedRestricts any modifications to the rule. Can only be unlocked by an application admin user.YNPublishedMakes the rule visible to other users within the same application.YYCacheTemporary files that your computer downloads and stores to save time in the future. YPublicMakes the rule visible in read-only mode to all users across the platform.YNScopeDatapod Scope Organization/Global/Application  PrefixA word, letter, or number placed before another.Y Key TypeWhen PHYSICAL, the primary key is enforced in the database, and when LOGICAL key information is stored within the metadata and not enforced in the databaseY Attribute PropertiesField NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault IDThe auto-generated sequence number of an attribute  KeyIndicates if the attribute is part of the primary key.  NNullIndicates if the attribute contains null values. NPartitionIf selected, a large datapod is divided into multiple partitions.  NActiveIndicates if the attribute is active. YPIIIndicates if the attribute contains PII ( Personally Identifiable Information ) data. NCDEIndicates if the attribute is a Critical Data Element. NHash KeyA hash key is a unique data identifier created by a hash function.  Business KeyA business key is a unique identifier for business entities or transactions.  NameName of the attribute in the database.  Display NameDisplay the name of the attribute.  TypeThe datatype of the attribute. eg. Varchar, Integer, etc.  LengthLength of the attribute.  PrecisionApplicable if the attribute type is Decimal.  ScaleApplicable if the attribute type is Decimal.  Unit TypeUnit of the data. eg. % Percent, $ Currency etc.  DescriptionDescription of the attribute.  DomainDomain of the data. eg. PAN, Passport, etc.  Additional Features:3. Show Meta CompareClick on the Meta Compare icon: This compares the datapod vs the actual table in the database and can identify inconsistencies. Datapod can be out of sync with the actual table if the table is modified from the back end.Open 4. Preview Click on the Preview icon:Preview the existing data in the datapod. You can also apply a filter on datapod and download the filtered data.Open  Open  5. Show Data StoreClick on Data Store:The Datastore keeps a log of each ingestion instance on the datapod.Open 6. Data ProfilingClick on the Data Profile icon.NOTE: To see the Data profiling we need to create and run the Data profiling rule on the respective datapod.Open 7. Data QualityClick on the Data Quality iconNOTE: To see the Data Quality report we need to create and run the Data Quality rule on the respective datapod or an attribute in the datapad.Open 8. Data LineageClick on Data LineageData lineage is the process of understanding, recording, and visualizing data as it flows from data sources to consumption. This includes all transformations the data underwent along the way—how the data was transformed, what changed, and why.Lineage is represented as a graph, typically it contains source and target entities in Data storage systems that are connected by a process invoked by a compute system.The highest of these tiers is an attribute-level lineage, which collects a high level of details about data. These details not only help document that the data has been well monitored and unaltered but can provide valuable insight to analysts to optimize the flow of data and find ways to improve the data platform.Entity level Open Attribute levelOpen Click > Action> ViewOpen 9. Show SQLDisplay the SQL Statement made at the backend for the definition of that Datapod.Open 10. Show DependenciesThis property is used to access the dependencies for that particular object. Open Download To Download Data Click --> DownloadOpen  12.Upload FileThe 'Upload File' feature allows users to directly insert data into a database table by uploading files (e.g., CSV, Excel). This streamlines data entry, enhancing efficiency and accuracy.Click --> Upload --> Action Open  Select File --> SubmitOpen  13.To add privileges for datapodClick -->  Privilege Icon On Right Corner1.User Level Privilege2.UserGroup Level Privilege Open Click --> Grant --> Add User for Privilege --> SubmitOpen 14.Help The 'Help' feature provides quick access to Inferyx documentation. By clicking the help icon, users are instantly navigated to relevant resources for support and guidance.Click --> Help IconOpen   , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseVizpodVizpodInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDatasetDatasetInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisRelease Notes - 6.4.3.1Release Notes - 6.4.3.1Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAccountAccountInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisKG ListKG ListInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisRelationRelationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

This feature empowers users with the ability to curate datasets tailored to their specific analytical needs. Users can selectively designate columns, implement filters, and introduce transformed columns within a dataset, mirroring the functionality of a structured query. This sophisticated capability underscores the platform's commitment to facilitating flexible and tailored data exploration, enabling users to construct datasets that align precisely with their analytical objectives.FeaturesList DatasetCreate DatasetPreview ResultsShow SQLData LineageShow Dependencies1.
