This versatile feature is applicable across various data structures, including Datapod, Dataset, or Relation. Users can seamlessly create expressions within these contexts, allowing for the formulation of intricate conditional conditions tailored to specific data processing requirements. This functionality not only enhances the flexibility of data manipulation but also contributes to the precision and sophistication of analytical operations performed on the platform.1. List Expression Open 2. Create Expression Click  -->  Add  Open 3. Configure Expressiona) Fill Necessary Field  -->  Click Submit           Open PropertiesField NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault UUIDUnique identifier of the expression  NameName of the expressionY Display NameDisplay the name of the expression.Y VersionThe version number for the expression. This is an audit column.   CreatedByThe user who created the expression. This is an audit column.  CreatedOnThe date and time when this expression is created. This is an audit column.  TagsAllows tagging of the meta. Tags can be used for searching metadata.N DescDescription of the expression.N DependsOnDATAPOD/DATASET/RELATIONY NameName of the Datapod/Dataset/RelationY ActiveActivate / De-Activate expression.YYLockedRestricts any modifications to the expression. Can only be unlocked by an application admin user.YNPublishedMakes the expression visible to other users within the same application.YYPublicMakes the expression visible in read-only mode to all users across the platform.YNLogical OperatorLogical operator of the filter. eg. AND, OR. This is applicable from the second condition onwards.  This is mandatory from the second condition onwards and the default is AND.  LHS TypeLeft-hand side element type. eg. Attribute, Expression, Formula, Integer, String, etc. AttributeLHS AttributeLeft-hand side attribute in the filter condition. eg. Attribute name in case the comparison is with attribute.  OperatorRelational operator in the condition. Eg. Equal, Not Equal, Less than, Greater than, etc. Equal (=)RHS TypeRight-hand side element type. eg. Attribute, Expression, Formula, Integer, String etc. AttributeRHS AttributeRight-hand side attribute in the filter condition. eg. Attribute name in case the comparison is with attribute.  Match TypeType of value to be returned if expression evaluation is true. eg. String, Formula  Match ValueThe actual value is to be returned if the evaluation of an expression is true. It can be of type string or formula as selected in Match Type.  Not Match TypeType of value to be returned if expression evaluation is false. eg. String, Formula  Not Match ValueThe actual value is to be returned if the evaluation of an expression is false. SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementData IngestionData QualityData ProfilingData PreparationDatapodDatasetExpressionFormulaFunctionMapMap RuleMap Rule GroupMap ResultsRelationParameter ListDM OperatorData ReconciliationData ArchivalData MigrationData VaultData PipelineBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Map RuleMore actionsMap RuleKiran P (Unlicensed)Yogesh PalrechaShreejit Deo (Unlicensed)Owned by Kiran P (Unlicensed)Last updated: Sept 10, 2024 by Yogesh Palrecha4 min readThe actual map rule is created to configure how the data is loaded from source to target.SCD2 MapThis mapping defines the process for handling historical changes to dimensional data  Using the Type SCD2  approach, this mapping captures changes to dimension attributes over time by maintaining historical records with effective date ranges. It facilitates the preservation of historical data integrity and enables the tracking of evolving dimensional values for accurate historical analysis and reporting. The mapping specifies how new records are inserted for changes, how end-dates are managed for expired records, and how current records are updated based on changing source data. This documentation provides insights into the logic and workflow of our SCD2 mapping strategy.Click Map --> Click RuleOpen 1.Map --> Click Add Open 3. Configure Map Rule Fill in the details -->  Select Type Of Map --> ContinueOpen  Type --> DefaultOpen 2.Type --> SCD2Open Click --> ContinueInfer dataset to Attribute Mapping.Open  Click --> Click SubmitOpen  PropertiesField NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault UUIDUnique identifier of the map rule  NameName of the map ruleY Display NameDisplay name of the map rule.Y VersionVersion number for the map rule. This is an audit column.   CreatedByThe user who created the map rule. This is an audit column.  CreatedOnThe date and time on which the map rule is created. This is an audit column.  TagsAllows tagging of the map rule. Tags can be used for searching metadata.N DescDescription of the map rule.N SourceSource type of the mapping. eg. Datapod, Dataset, Relation.  NameName of the Datapod, Dataset or Relation from which the data will be loaded to target.  TargetType of the Target to which the data will be loaded from source. eg. Datapod  NameName of the Datapod to which the data will be loaded from the source.  ActiveActivate / De-Activate the map rule.YYSource HashkeyHashkey Column Of The Sorce Table   Target HashkeyHashkey Column Of The Target Table   LockedRestricts any modifications to the map rule. Can only be unlocked by application admin user.YNPublishedMakes the map rule visible to other users within the same application.YYPublicMakes the map rule visible in read only mode to all users across the platform.YNTypeType Of Map Rule DEFAULT/SCD2  Save ModeMode in which the data will be loaded.          eg. Append: The data will be appended on top of existing data.                                                                                  Merge: The data will be updated if already exists or inserted if does not exist in target.                                  Overwrite: The data will be refreshed or overwritten  over existing data.  Merge ConditionCondition to be used for Merge mode. This is the join criteria on key columns between source and target to check if the data exists in the target.  Job NotificationsOption to notify upon different statuses of map rule like Starting, Completed, Pause, Running, Failed, Killed etc.   ActiveIndicates whether the attribute mapping is active or not.  Source TypeType of source eg. Attribute, Expression, Formula, Function etc.  Source AttributesName of the attribute/expression from which the data will be loaded.  Target AttributesName of the target attribute in which the data will be loaded.  Filter PropertiesField NameDescriptionMandatoryDefaultField NameDescriptionMandatoryDefaultFilter GroupFor grouping filter conditions  Logical OperatorFor combining two or more relational statements. AND/OR  LHS TypeType of LHS component(Attribute, Expression, Formula, Integer, String )  LHS AttributeName of LHS component  Operatoroperation to be established between LHS Attribute and RHS Attribute  RHS TypeType of RHS component(Attribute, Expression, Formula, Integer, String etc.)  RHS AttributeName of RHS component  Additional Features:Click Preview  Open   Click Show Dependencies Open Show SQLOpen Data Lineage Data lineage is the process of understanding, recording, and visualizing data as it flows from data sources to consumption. This includes all transformations the data underwent along the way—how the data was transformed, what changed, and why.Lineage is represented as a graph, typically it contains source and target entities in Data storage systems that are connected by a process invoked by a compute system.The highest of these tiers is an attribute-level lineage, which collects a high level of details about data. These details not only help document that the data has been well monitored and unaltered but can provide valuable insight to analysts to optimize the flow of data and find ways to improve the data platform.Entity levelOpen Attribute LevelOpen  Click Action --> ViewOpen   , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseMap Rule GroupMap Rule GroupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisRule GroupRule GroupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisRuleRuleInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisEntity RuleEntity RuleInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDQ RuleDQ RuleInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisDP RuleDP RuleInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementData IngestionData QualityData ProfilingData PreparationData ReconciliationDR RuleDR Rule GroupDR ResultCompare ResultsDR Parameter ListData ArchivalData MigrationData VaultData PipelineBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Data ReconciliationMore actionsData ReconciliationYogesh PalrechaSukalyan Hazari (Unlicensed)Pavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Owned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Dec 19, 20231 min readIntroductionThroughout the process of data migration, challenges may arise due to inaccuracies in mapping and transformation logic, as well as unforeseen runtime failures like network disruptions or incomplete transactions. These issues can result in the data being left in an inconsistent or invalid state, manifesting various complications such as:Missing RecordsMissing ValuesIncorrect ValuesDuplicated RecordsBadly Formatted ValuesBroken Relationships Across Tables or SystemsThese discrepancies, if left unaddressed, pose a significant threat to the overall accuracy of the data, potentially leading to misleading insights and impacting customer service quality. Recognizing the critical need for meticulous data reconciliation, organizations can identify, rectify, and validate these discrepancies, ensuring the integrity and reliability of the migrated data. The data reconciliation stage plays a pivotal role in maintaining the highest standards of data quality and precision throughout the migration process.DR Rule.DR Rule Group.DR Result.Compare Results.DR Parameter Lst  , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseData ArchivalData ArchivalInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisData ManagementData ManagementInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData PreparationData PreparationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData QualityData QualityInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData MigrationData MigrationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDatasetDatasetInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisAtlassian Intelligence

The archived data comprises information that retains significance for the organization, serving as a valuable resource for potential future reference. Additionally, data archiving addresses the imperative of retaining specific datasets in compliance with regulatory standards and obligations. This strategic approach to data management optimizes the utilization of primary storage resources while safeguarding the accessibility and integrity of historical data, aligning with organizational, legal, and compliance requirements.DA RuleDA Rule GroupDA Rule ResultsDA Parameter List , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseData ManagementData ManagementInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisData QualityData QualityInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisDatastoreDatastoreInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData ScienceData ScienceInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDatasetDatasetInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData PipelineData PipelineInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisAtlassian Intelligence

SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementData IngestionData QualityData ProfilingData PreparationData ReconciliationData ArchivalDA RuleDA Rule GroupDA Rule ResultsDA Parameter ListData MigrationData VaultData PipelineBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/DA RuleMore actionsDA RuleYogesh PalrechaKiran P (Unlicensed)Owned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Sept 19, 20243 min readData archiving is a systematic procedure involving the relocation of data that is no longer in active use to dedicated storage, ensuring its preservation for long-term retention. The archived data comprises information that retains significance for the organization, serving as a valuable resource for potential future reference. Additionally, data archiving addresses the imperative of retaining specific datasets in compliance with regulatory standards and obligations. This strategic approach to data management optimizes the utilization of primary storage resources while safeguarding the accessibility and integrity of historical data, aligning with organizational, legal, and compliance requirements.Users have the flexibility to choose between two archival types:FULL: Archives all data from the source datapod to the target datapod.Incremental: Archives only the incremental changes since the last archival operation. Users can select an incremental key attribute to determine which data should be considered for archiving based on changes.In the target datapod, an audit key attribute can be defined to track and identify archived data. This attribute serves as a reference point for auditing and ensures the integrity and traceability of the archived data.Along with the audit key attribute, an audit value is associated with each archived record. This value provides additional context or metadata related to the archival operation, aiding in data management and analysis.1. Click Data Archival  --> Click RuleOpen    2. title: DM Rule - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
DM Rule - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementData IngestionData QualityData ProfilingData PreparationData ReconciliationData ArchivalData MigrationDM RuleDM Rule ResultsData VaultData PipelineBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/DM RuleMore actionsDM RuleYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Sept 18, 20243 min readMigration can occur between source and target databases located in any environment, like on-premises or different clouds. Each source and target database can be in a different environment; not all need to be collocated in the same environment. Data migration offers flexibility and control to users, allowing them to tailor the migration process according to their specific requirements and constraints. It ensures efficient transfer of data between different data sources while minimizing downtime and disruptions to business operations.The process you've described involves migrating data between two different data sources, allowing users to select a list of tables to be migrated. Here's a breakdown of the steps involved:Users are provided with an interface where they can select which tables they want to migrate from the source data to the destination. Instead of migrating all the data from a table at once, the migration process is divided into batches. This approach helps in managing large volumes of data more efficiently, reduces the risk of errors, and allows for better monitoring and control of the migration process. Users can apply filters at the table level to specify which data should be migrated. This filtering capability allows for selective migration of data based on criteria such as date ranges, specific columns, or other conditions.Users have the option to configure the migration process to either run tables in parallel or sequentially.In parallel migration, multiple tables are migrated simultaneously. This can significantly reduce the overall migration time, especially when dealing with a large number of tables.In sequential migration, tables are migrated one after the other. While this approach may take longer compared to parallel migration, it may be preferred in certain scenarios where resource constraints or dependencies between tables exist.Users can specify the number of parallel threads to be used during the migration process. Each thread is responsible for migrating a specific portion of data, allowing for efficient utilization of system resources and optimizing the migration performance.1. Click Data Migration  --> Click RuleOpen 2. Click Data Migration  --> Click Rule --> AddOpen Configure Data Migration Rulea) Fill in the necessary fields  --> Click Continue Open b) Fill in the necessary fields  --> Click ContinueOpen To configure the filter condition click edit at row levelOpen c) Fill in the necessary fields  --> Click SubmitOpen PropertiesField NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault UUIDUnique identifier of the Rule  NameName of the RuleY Display NameDisplay the name of the RuleY VersionThe version number for the Rule. title: Data Pipeline - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Data Pipeline - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementData IngestionData QualityData ProfilingData PreparationData ReconciliationData ArchivalData MigrationData VaultData PipelineCreate PipelinePipeline ResultsPipeline Parameter ListBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Data PipelineMore actionsData PipelineYogesh PalrechaPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Owned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Dec 19, 20231 min readIntroductionA data pipeline represents a structured sequence of data processing stages. Initiated by the ingestion of data, if not already present in the data platform, the pipeline encompasses a series of interconnected steps, where each step produces an output serving as the input for subsequent stages. This sequential flow persists until the entirety of the pipeline operations is concluded. Notably, certain pipelines incorporate the execution of independent steps in parallel, enhancing efficiency.Integral to data pipelines are three fundamental components: a source, one or more processing steps, and a destination—sometimes referred to as a sink. The destination serves as the endpoint for the processed data, be it a data warehouse, analytics database, or another designated repository. Data pipelines facilitate the seamless transfer of data between applications, from data lakes to analytics databases, exemplifying their versatility. Additionally, in scenarios where the source and destination coincide, the pipeline becomes a mechanism for transformative modifications to the dataset. Essentially, whenever data undergoes processing between distinct points, a data pipeline orchestrates this journey, optimizing data flow and enabling efficient data manipulation. title: Business Rule Engine - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Business Rule Engine - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineBusiness RulesAlert GenerationCase ManagementAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Business Rule EngineMore actionsBusiness Rule EngineYogesh PalrechaPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Owned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Dec 18, 20231 min readINTRODUCTIONA business rules engine (BRE) is an application that manages decision processes using pre-defined logic to determine outcomes. BREs enable precise decision-making and are especially useful for complex dependencies, as well as in instances where regulatory or organizational rule changes frequently require logic changes. Automation can mean the difference between rule changes taking months vs. hours. They can also help companies avoid significant fines and penalties for falling out of compliance.The Inferyx platform has infused rules in an interactive development scenario. The built-in case alert enables you to create business scenarios by defining custom criteria, and thresholds and to generate alerts and scoring.Especially for an event monitoring scenario rule engine that can be used for a batch process over data. Also, to fully automate interactions in a workflow by having a set of rules to automatically make workflow decisions.Some examples of Business rules.Every day millions of decisions are being made. We don’t realize that many times rules govern what decision should be made and when. Yet, each business system must apply the proper decision logic to each task to reach the desired outcome at the transaction level.Some examples could include:Health insurance companies need to decide if a potential new customer meets eligibility requirementsFinancial institutions must verify that a loan meets all requirements and guidelines for insurance, paperwork, and regulations to mitigate risk and maintain compliance with numerous and constantly changing state and federal regulations, corporate policies, and customer expectationsRetailers need to determine which customers get free shipping and if payments should be heldHow do Business Rules Engines deliver business value?Keep pace with marketplace changesBoost efficiency and productivityEnsure regulatory and policy complianceImprove customer serviceOpen new revenue streams, multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseBusiness RulesBusiness RulesInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisRule GroupRule GroupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisEntity RuleEntity RuleInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAG Rule GroupAG Rule GroupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDR Rule GroupDR Rule GroupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisRuleRuleInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineBusiness RulesAlert GenerationCase ManagementCM RuleCM Rule GroupCM Rule ResultCM AnalysisAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Case ManagementMore actionsCase ManagementYogesh PalrechaPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Owned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: May 11, 2022 by Pavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)INTRODUCTIONCase management is the work—recording, monitoring and analysis—involved in the processing of the data, procedures, and related content that comprise a case.A case can be an investigation that must be conducted, a service request that must be fulfilled or an incident or issue that must be resolved.As opposed to business process management (BPM), which focuses specifically on workflows and repeatable processes, case management is a more holistic technique for managing work and processes that aren’t repeatable. Case management solutions are designed to improve knowledge-driven work as well as to improve general processes to optimize outcomes., multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseData ManagementData ManagementInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisBusiness RulesBusiness RulesInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisBusiness Rule EngineBusiness Rule EngineInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisWorkflow ManagerWorkflow ManagerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDP Rule GroupDP Rule GroupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAG RuleAG RuleInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisAtlassian Intelligence

SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsEntity ResolutionData ScienceAlgorithmDistributionOperatorModelTrainingPredictionGenerative AIEvaluationSimulationResultsModel DeployFeatureKnowledge GraphWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/AlgorithmMore actionsAlgorithmPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Pavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Last updated: Sept 20, 2024 by Yogesh Palrecha3 min readThe algorithm is a set of well-defined instructions to solve a particular problem. The algorithm is part of the data science module.How to design an Algorithm:To write an algorithm, the following things are needed as pre-requisite:The problem that is to be solved by this algorithm.2. The problem's constraints must be considered while solving the problem.3. The input to be taken to solve the problem.4. The output to be expected when the problem is solved.5. The solution to this problem, in the given constraints.Use of algorithm in data science model:In the context of data science models, algorithms serve as sophisticated mathematical models and mapping techniques. These computational tools are instrumental in unraveling hidden patterns within datasets. Machine learning, a subset of data science, employs a diverse set of algorithms capable of tasks such as pattern recognition, classification, and prediction. These algorithms learn and adapt by analyzing existing data, contributing to the model's ability to make informed decisions and generate valuable insights.In the Inferyx platform, we integrate a variety of machine learning algorithms, encompassing both supervised and unsupervised learning approaches. Additionally, advanced analytics benefit from the incorporation of deep learning algorithms, enhancing the platform's capabilities to handle complex data patterns and relationships. This integration empowers users to perform in-depth analyses and gain actionable insights from their data, making Inferyx a robust solution for data-driven decision-making in diverse domains.Creating Algorithm 1. Click Data Science  --> Click Algorithm Open 2. Click  --> Add Open 3. Configure Algorithma) Fill in the necessary fields  -->  Click SubmitOpen PropertiesField NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault  Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault  UUIDUnique identifier of the algorithm.   NameName of the algorithm.Y  Display NameDisplay the name of the algorithm.Y  VersionVersion number for the algorithm. Y  Customer Flagpersonalized with user scripts.YY Script PathPath of  algorithm scriptY  Train ClassThe sample of data used to fit the model.Y  Model Classmodel class organizes data elements and standardizes how the data elements relate to one another.Y  Label RequiredThe label is basically a Target column.             YY Summary MethodsA summary puts into concise form the essential information or ideas.   Default Param ListThe Param List object contains all saved parameter values of the Script. Pacific param list for Pacific algorithm.   Param List(Hyper Param)Hyperparameter tuning is choosing a set of optimal hyperparameters for a learning algorithm.Y  Feature Weightfeature weight, which seeks to estimate the relative importance of each feature.YY SamplingSampling is a process used in statistical analysis in which a predetermined number of observations are taken from a larger population.YY Train Test SplitSplit arrays or matrices into random train and test subsets.YY Hyper ParamsHyperparameter tuning is choosing a set of optimal hyperparameters for a learning algorithm. N StatusCheck the validity of the meta.   Click  --> Action  --> ViewOpen  , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapsePredictionPredictionInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisGenerative AIGenerative AIInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisData ScienceData ScienceInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisFunctionFunctionInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisEvaluationEvaluationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisModelModelInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Model - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Model - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsEntity ResolutionData ScienceAlgorithmDistributionOperatorModelTrainingPredictionGenerative AIEvaluationSimulationResultsModel DeployFeatureKnowledge GraphWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/ModelMore actionsModelPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Pavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Last updated: Sept 20, 2024 by Yogesh Palrecha3 min readMachine learning models are computational algorithms designed to enable systems to learn patterns, make predictions, and perform tasks without being explicitly programmed. These models leverage statistical techniques to analyze and interpret data, uncovering intricate patterns and relationships. In the context of data science, machine learning models are utilized for various purposes, including classification, regression, clustering, and recommendation.To deploy a machine learning model:Training Phase: The model is exposed to a labeled dataset, allowing it to learn and adapt to the underlying patterns within the data. During this phase, the model adjusts its parameters to optimize performance.Testing and Validation: The trained model is evaluated using a separate dataset to assess its accuracy and generalizability. This step ensures that the model can make reliable predictions on new, unseen data.Deployment: Once validated, the model is deployed for real-world use. It takes input data, processes it using the learned patterns, and produces predictions or classifications, contributing valuable insights to decision-making processes.Machine learning models can range from traditional algorithms like linear regression to more complex ones such as neural networks in deep learning. The choice of the model depends on the nature of the problem, the characteristics of the data, and the desired outcomes. Regular evaluation and, if necessary, retraining are essential to maintain model accuracy as data patterns evolve over time.The platform's flexibility allows users to train models on labeled data as well as unlabeled data, ensuring adaptability to unique business challenges. With a comprehensive training, testing, and validation framework, Using the Inferyx platform we can ensure the reliability and accuracy of deployed machine learning models. Click Data Science --> Click ModelOpen  2.  Click Add Open   3. Fill in the necessary fields --> ContinueOpen  PropertiesField NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault UUIDUnique identifier of the model.  NameName of the model.Y Display NameDisplay name of the model.Y VersionVersion number for the model. This is an audit column.   CreatedByThe user who created the model. This is an audit column.  Created OnThe date and time of creation.  TagsTags can be used for searching metadata.  DescDescription of the model.  ActiveActivate / De-Activate the model. YLockedRestricts any modifications to the model. After undergoing the training process, during which the model learns patterns from labeled data, it becomes capable of generalizing its knowledge to make predictions on previously unseen instances. The steps involved in model prediction are as follows:Input Data:Provide new, unseen data to the trained machine learning model. This data should have the same features as the training data but lacks corresponding target labels.Preprocessing:Apply the same preprocessing steps used during training to ensure consistency and compatibility between the training and prediction phases.Handle missing values, normalize or scale features, and perform any necessary transformations.Model Inference:Feed the preprocessed input data into the trained model.The model utilizes the patterns learned during training to generate predictions or classifications for the new data.Output:Obtain the model's predictions as output.The predictions can take various forms depending on the nature of the problem, such as class labels in classification tasks or numerical values in regression tasks.Evaluation:Evaluate the model's predictions using appropriate metrics to assess its accuracy and reliability.This step helps confirm the model's generalization capabilities and its performance on real-world data.1. The goal is to understand how well the model generalizes to new, unseen data and to identify any areas for improvement. The following steps are typically involved in model evaluation:Test Data Set:Separate a portion of the labeled data (not used during training) to create a test set. This set serves as a proxy for real-world, unseen data.Prediction:Use the trained model to make predictions on the test set. The model applies what it learned during training to new instances and generates output.Evaluation Metrics:Choose appropriate evaluation metrics based on the nature of the problem. Common metrics include accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, and area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC-ROC), among others.Model evaluation is an ongoing process, especially as new data becomes available or the model is applied to different contexts. It ensures that machine learning models contribute meaningful and reliable insights to decision-making processes. 1. Click Data Science --> Click Evaluation Open 2. Click Add Open  3. SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsEntity ResolutionData ScienceAlgorithmDistributionOperatorModelTrainingPredictionGenerative AIEvaluationSimulationResultsModel DeployFeatureKnowledge GraphWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/ResultsMore actionsResultsPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Yogesh PalrechaOwned by Pavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Last updated: Dec 19, 2023 by Yogesh Palrecha3 min readA Result section is employed to scrutinize the outcomes of various tasks, including training, evaluation, prediction, and operator execution. For instance, the results from the training phase encompass feature importance, evaluation metrics, as well as samples from both the training and test datasets, along with feature weights. This section provides a comprehensive overview of the performance and key insights derived from each job, facilitating a thorough analysis of the entire process.1. Click Data Science > Click Results Open 1. Type: Training      Click Action  --> Click View Open    Results          1. Click Summarysummary is a summarization of task settings such as algorithm used, number of records, model parameters, etc. Open  Click Feature Importance Feature importance refers to a concept in machine learning that quantifies the impact or contribution of different input features (variables) in predicting the output or target variable. Understanding feature importance is crucial for interpreting the model's behavior, identifying influential factors, and gaining insights into the underlying patterns in the data. The significance of feature importance varies depending on the specific algorithm used for modeling.Open Click Performance performance is a model performance metrics or evaluation metrics that refers to quantitative measures used to assess the performance of a model. These metrics provide insights into how well a model is making predictions and help to gauge its effectiveness in solving a specific problem. Different types of metrics are employed based on the nature of the task, such as classification, regression, or clustering. Common evaluation metrics include:1. Classification Metrics:1. Accuracy: The proportion of correctly classified instances.2. Precision: The ratio of true positives to the sum of true positives and false positives, measuring the accuracy of positive predictions.3. Recall (Sensitivity): The ratio of true positives to the sum of true positives and false negatives, indicating the model's ability to capture all relevant instances.4. F1 Score: The harmonic mean of precision and recall, offering a balance between the two.5. Regression Metrics:Mean Absolute Error (MAE): The average absolute difference between predicted and actual values.Mean Squared Error (MSE): The average squared difference between predicted and actual values, giving more weight to larger errors.Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE): The square root of the MSE, providing an interpretable scale.Open Click Train DataIt is a sample of data used for training a machine-learning model.Open Click Test DataIt is a sample of data used for testing a machine-learning model.Open Click Feature Weight  --> Select Row Identifier Feature weight is a row-wise measure of feature importance, indicating the weight assigned to each feature when predicting or classifying the label for a specific row or record.Chart View Open Grid ViewOpen    , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseEvaluationEvaluationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDQ ResultsDQ ResultsInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisModelModelInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDatasetDatasetInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisPredictionPredictionInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData ScienceData ScienceInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Feature - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Feature - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsEntity ResolutionData ScienceAlgorithmDistributionOperatorModelTrainingPredictionGenerative AIEvaluationSimulationResultsModel DeployFeatureKnowledge GraphWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/FeatureMore actionsFeatureYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Sept 20, 20242 min readA feature  is a logical table of features that provides a single API for updating feature values, two different APIs - an online and an offline API - for reading feature values. In practice, features are stored in a dual database system with an online database storing the latest feature values in row-oriented table format  (e.g., OLTP databases or key-value stores) for fast feature retrieval during online inference, and the second offline database stores the historical feature values in a column-oriented store (e.g., data warehouse/lakehouse or object store). The offline API (to the offline store) is used to create training data and batch inference data. The data between the online and offline stores should be kept consistent by the feature store.A feature  has an entity_id (or primary key), and optionally an event_time column (indicating when the features in that row were observed), and a partition_key (to layout the data in the offline store for faster query performance). Each row in the online store is uniquely identified by its entity_id (as the online store only stores the latest values for entities), but the offline store stores historical feature values for entities, so each row is uniquely identified by the (entity_id, event_time) pair. For example, for a credit card, the online store would only store the latest transaction feature data (with the credit card number as the entity_id), but the offline store would store all the historical transactions for that credit card - each transaction is uniquely identified by the credit card number and the timestamp for that transaction.The other columns in a features are the features. You can select a particular feature (column) as the partition key for a feature  to determine how to layout the rows in the offline store such that you can efficiently query the data using queries with the partition_key. For example, if your partition key is the day and you have hundreds of days worth of data, with a day partition_key, you can query for a given day or a range of days, and only the data for those days will be read from disk (a push-down filter).List - Feature listGroup - feature group Click Data Science -->Click Feature---->Click Group--->Click Add Open Filled all the required field and click---continueOpen PropertiesField NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault UUIDUnique identifier of the training.  NameName of the traning.Y Display NameDisplay name of the training.Y VersionVersion number for the training. title: Knowledge Graph - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Knowledge Graph - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsEntity ResolutionData ScienceKnowledge GraphKG ListKG ResultsAnalysisKG Parameter ListWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Knowledge GraphMore actionsKnowledge GraphYogesh PalrechaPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Owned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: May 11, 2022 by Pavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)1 min readINTRODUCTIONA knowledge graph, also known as a semantic network, represents a network of real-world entities,i.e. objects, events, situations, or concepts,and illustrates the relationship between them. This information is usually stored in a graph database and visualized as a graph structure, prompting the term knowledge “graph.”A knowledge graph is made up of three main components: nodes, edges, and labels. Any object, place, or person can be a node. An edge defines the relationship between the nodes.Use cases of knowledge graphsThere are a number of popular, consumer-facing knowledge graphs, which are setting user expectations for search systems across enterprises. Some of these knowledge graphs include:Retail: Knowledge graphs have been for up-sell and cross-sell strategies, recommending products based on individual purchase behavior and popular purchase trends across demographic groups.Entertainment: Knowledge graphs are also leveraged for artificial intelligence (AI) based recommendation engines for content platforms, like Netflix, SEO, or social media. Based on click and other online engagement behaviors, these providers recommend new content for users to read or watch.Finance: This technology has also been used for know-your-customer (KYC) and anti-money laundering initiatives within the finance industry. They assist in financial crime prevention and investigation, allowing banking institutions to understand the flow of money across their clientele and identify noncompliant customers.Healthcare: Knowledge graphs are also benefiting the healthcare industry by organizing and categorizing relationships within medical research. This information assists providers by validating diagnoses and identifying treatment plans based on individual needs.  , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseRelationRelationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData ScienceData ScienceInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAlgorithmAlgorithmInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisGenerative AIGenerative AIInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisEntityEntityInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisModelModelInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Workflow Manager - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Workflow Manager - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerWFM ListWFM ResultBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Workflow ManagerMore actionsWorkflow ManagerYogesh PalrechaPavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)Owned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: May 11, 2022 by Pavan Kulkarni (Unlicensed)1 min readINTRODUCTIONWorkflow management is the coordination of tasks that make up the work an organization does. By ‘workflow’ we mean a sequence of tasks that are part of some larger task, and is sometimes synonymous with ‘business process’. The purpose of a workflow is to achieve some result, and the purpose of workflow management is to achieve better results according to some set of goals.Workflow management is principally a management discipline, focused on the structure of work within some organization and how teams collaborate to complete this work. In principle, workflow management doesn’t require software, but in practice software tools are used to keep track of the work and to automate parts of it.Workflow management is concerned with the work that people do, but tasks may also be automated and performed by IT systems – computer software. Workflow management can therefore include IT systems integration, in order to share data between workflows and other IT systems within an organization.The visibility of the work that comes with workflow management tends to lead to awareness of how to improve these workflows. Business process improvement is a natural part of workflow management. This is also one reason why workflow management is a management discipline and not just software: process improvement is about changing how people do their jobs, which is harder than reconfiguring software., multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseAnalytics WorkbenchAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisWFM ListWFM ListInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData ScienceData ScienceInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDP Rule GroupDP Rule GroupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisCreate PipelineCreate PipelineInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDatapodDatapodInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisAtlassian Intelligence

Most batch schedulers support multiple operating systems and platforms. Batch schedulers, also known as job schedulers, support the following high-level functions:Defining batch jobs and batch schedules.Defining batch calendars.Defining batch nodes and node groups (servers).Executing and controlling batch schedules and batch jobs.Monitoring schedules and job execution (including alerts and re-submission).Recycling batch schedules for the next run date., multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseBatchBatchInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisSchedulerSchedulerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisJob MonitoringJob MonitoringInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisWorkflow ManagerWorkflow ManagerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisWFM ListWFM ListInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisWFM ResultWFM ResultInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

Concretely, a data source may be a database, a flat file, live measurements from physical devices, scraped web data, or any of the myriad static and streaming data services that abound across the internet.Click Admin > Click Data Source Open 2. Click > AddOpen  3. Configure the Source a) Fill in the necessary fields> Click SubmitOpen PropertiesField NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault UUIDUnique identifier of the Datasource  NameName of the DatasourceY Display NameDisplay name of the DatasourceY VersionVersion number for the Datasource. This is an audit column.   CreatedByThe user who created the Datasource. This is an audit column.  TagsAllows tagging of the meta. Tags can be used for searching metadata.N DescDescription of the DatasourceN ActiveActivate / De-Activate the DatasourceYYLockedRestricts any modifications to the Datasource. Can only be unlocked by application admin user.YNPublishedMakes the Datasource visible to other users within the same application.YYCachetemporary files that your computer downloads and stores to save time in the future. YPublicMakes the Datasource visible in read only mode to all users across the platform.YNApplicationSelect the appliction to which you are granting the datasource  Categorycategory of the datas ource, RDBMS/SPARK/FILE/API etcY TypeType of category with respect to the selected categoryY Formatwhen we select FILE category we need to select the file format, CSV/EXCEL/JSON etc  Auth Typetype of authentication , NONE/BASIC.  URLenter the url  Accessenter the access e.g JDBC  Driverenter the driver for jdbc  Hosthost of database  Portport number of database  DB Namedatabase name  Schema Nameschema name in the database  Username user name of the database  Passwordpassword of the database  Pathpath   Session Parametersparameter to run the session, e.g.useSSL=false  Connectionclick on Test button to test the connection.  ActionCheck the validity of meta  Click > Action > ViewOpen Source Connector TypesCategoryConnector TypeCategoryConnector TypeRDBMSOracle, MySQL, PostGres, MSSQL, Hive, Snowflake, Athena, DB2, DataBricks, Impala, Kudu, RedShift, SQLLiteGraphDBNeo4JDocDBMongoDBAPIRestFeatureStoreHopsWorksStreamKafkaFileLocal FileSystem, S3 Bucket, FTP Server, Google Sheet , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseDatastoreDatastoreInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDatasetDatasetInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData ManagementData ManagementInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisResourceResourceInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisEntityEntityInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisModelModelInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Bulk Export Import - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Bulk Export Import - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAccountActivityApplicationCalendarLOVCategoryDatasourceDatastoreDomainNotification TemplateOrganizationResourceSessionSysparamSecurityWebserviceRegister SourceMigration AssistExport ImportBulk Export ImportServer SettingsApplication SettingsFile ManagerPredict ServerServerServiceSystem MonitoringJob MonitoringMetadata NavigatorMetadataNotificationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Bulk Export ImportMore actionsBulk Export ImportYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Oct 08, 20243 min readYou can migrate your meta from one application to another with  help of Migration Assistant .1. Login to System adminOpen the Inferyx platform and log in using credentials.Select the Inferyx System Admin application.Choose the admin role to proceed.2. Accessing Migration AssistantNavigate to the Admin menu.Select the Migration Assist option.3. Single Export Process (Mandatory for Bulk Export)By Default Bulk Export set to No for single export Open Add Export Process:Click on the Add button.Enter the Name for the export.Click Continue.Open Advanced Search:In the Advanced Search section, select the Type (e.g., Application, Account, Organization, Group, User).Export system data in bulk, using specific system labels such as Application, Account, Organization, Group, and User. The data is exported separately by label, followed by the bulk export of organisations.Click Select to proceed.Open Click Continue to proceed.Open Submit and Run:Click Submit.Select Run Immediately.Open Check Logs and Download File:Check for any errors or issues in the Logs section.Once completed, download the exported file from the logs.Open Note: Single Import must be completed before starting the Bulk Import. 4. Bulk Export ProcessSelect Bulk Export:Set Bulk Export to Yes.Open Add Export Process:Click on the Add button.Open 3. Select Export Entities:Choose the required Organization, Account, and Application for the bulk export.Open Click Submit and Continue  to proceed.Select Run Immediately.Open Check Logs and Download File:Monitor the progress in the Logs section.Once completed, go to the logs and download the exported file.Open 5. Import Process on Another Server Note- same import process for single and bulk import Once the export is completed, you can import the data on the target server:Login to sys_admin on the target server:Open the Inferyx platform on the target server.Log in using sys_admin credentials.Access Migration Assistant:Navigate to Admin > Migration Assist.Select the Import option.Add Import Process:Click on the Add button. Enter the Name of the import process.Open Click Continue.Select Exported File:Choose the file you downloaded during the export process.Open Click Continue.Submit and Run:Click Submit.Select Run Immediately.Check Logs:Go to Logs and Monitor the logs to check the status of the import.Ensure the import is completed successfully by reviewing the log file., multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseExport ImportExport ImportInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisProject  Deployment ProcessProject  Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisPatch Deployment ProcessPatch Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData ManagementData ManagementInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisOverviewOverviewInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Analytics Workbench - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Analytics Workbench - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchSQL ManagerFile UploadNotebook ManagerInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Analytics WorkbenchMore actionsAnalytics WorkbenchYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Mar 27, 20251 min readIntroductionThe Analytics Workbench provides a powerful and intuitive platform for data analysis and exploration, designed to meet the needs of data professionals and analysts. It encompasses three core modules: SQL Manager, File Upload, and Notebook, each tailored to streamline analytical workflows. The SQL Manager module offers robust capabilities for querying and managing data across various databases, enabling users to execute complex SQL operations with ease. The File Upload module facilitates the seamless import of data in diverse formats—such as CSVs, spreadsheets, and other files—into the workbench for processing and analysis. The Notebook module delivers an interactive environment where users can write and execute code,document their analytical processes, enhancing the platform’s flexibility and depth. Together, these modules can be leveraged independently or orchestrated into a unified analytics pipeline, providing precision and adaptability for a wide range of data-driven tasks.Login – >Open 2.Select the application -->Open Click the links below-1.SQL Manager2.File Manager3.Notebook Manager , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseDashboardDashboardInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData ProfilingData ProfilingInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisNotebook ManagerNotebook ManagerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisSQL ManagerSQL ManagerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDatasetDatasetInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisData VisualizationData VisualizationInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

main_content:

SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchSQL ManagerFile UploadNotebook ManagerInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/SQL ManagerMore actionsSQL ManagerYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaMar 21, 20252 min readTo access and query data sources in Analytics Workbench, navigate to SQL Manager, where multiple data sources are available. Before running queries, ensure that your user account has the necessary privileges; otherwise, the data sources will not be visible or accessible. Once the required permissions are granted, you can write and execute queries on any available data source.1. Click--> SQL Manager--> Select Datasource in ContextOpen 2.Click on Datasource to display the tables created under that data source. After selecting a table, its columns will be displayed. Click on the three-dot menu of the table and select Insert into Editor to generate a SELECT statement for that table.Open 3.Click--> the Run button in the top-right corner to execute the query. After execution, the query results will be displayed in the Result tab, and the query logs will also be available for review.Click on the Plus button to add multiple tabs, allowing you to execute multiple queries simultaneously.Open 4.Click --> on the History button in the top-right corner to view the list of previously executed queries.Open PropertiesField NameDescriptionField NameDescriptionHomeHome page of SQL ManagerAddCreate multiple SQL tabsHistoryQuery History pageRefresh Refresh the SQL tabRunRun can be used for Execute querySQL FormatUsed for format the query Copy Script Used for Copy the ScriptHide DatasourcesCan be used for Hide Datasources InformationMaximizeUsed for Maximize SQL tabDownload scriptCan be used for Download the script , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseAnalytics WorkbenchAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDatasetDatasetInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDashboardDashboardInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAnalysisAnalysisInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisReportReportInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisFile UploadFile UploadInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

main_content:

SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchSQL ManagerFile UploadNotebook ManagerInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Inferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/File UploadMore actionsFile UploadYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaMar 21, 20252 min readTo upload and manage data in Analytics Workbench, navigate to the File Upload module, where multiple file formats are supported. Before uploading files, ensure that your user account has the necessary permissions; otherwise, the upload functionality will not be accessible. Once the required access is granted, you can seamlessly import and manage data files within the platform.Open 1.Click --> on the Plus button to create a new folder.  Click on Create Open 2.Click --> on the Delete button to remove a file or folder. Multiple files or folders can be deleted at once by selecting them and then clicking Delete.Open 3.Click on the Download button to download a file or folder. Multiple files or folders can be downloaded at once by selecting them and then clicking Download.Click on OkOpen 4.Select a folder first, then click on the Upload button to upload files into the selected folder. Multiple files can be uploaded at once.Open PropertiesField NameDescriptionField NameDescriptionAddUsed for Create FolderDeleteCan be used for delete files or folderDownload Can be used for Download FilesUploadUpload can be used for upload files in folderRefresh Refresh the file upload module , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseRelease Notes - 6.2.2.3Release Notes - 6.2.2.3Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisCreate PipelineCreate PipelineInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisRelease Notes - 6.2.4Release Notes - 6.2.4Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisSQL ManagerSQL ManagerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisWFM ListWFM ListInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisNotebook ManagerNotebook ManagerInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

5.x/Notebook ManagerMore actionsNotebook ManagerYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Mar 27, 20252 min readInferyx Workbench provides seamless integration with Jupyter and Zeppelin notebooks, enabling data scientists and analysts to perform interactive data analysis, visualization, and model development.Jupyter Notebook Integration 1.Interactive Code Execution: Run code in small, manageable cells with instant feedback.2.Rich Text Support: Use Markdown to add headings, lists, and formatted text.3.Data Visualization: Integrate libraries like Matplotlib, Seaborn, and Plotly for interactive graphs.4.Notebook Sharing: Export notebooks in formats like HTML, PDF, or Python scripts.Zeppelin Notebook Integration1.Supports multi-language data processing, including SQL, Scala, and Python.2.Ideal for big data analytics and Apache Spark-based computations.3.Allows collaborative and interactive data exploration.4.Provides built-in visualization tools for insightful data representation.1.Click --> on Notebook to view the list of all created notebooks.Open 2.Click --> Add Fill in the necessary fields --> Click SubmitOpen 3.Click on the Action button on the notebook list page, select Launch, and then click OK to proceed.Open 4.After clicking OK, you will be directly navigated to the Jupyter Notebook.Open PropertiesField NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault Field NameDescriptionMandatoryDefault UUIDUnique identifier of the Notebook  NameName of the NotebookY Display NameDisplay name of the NotebookY VersionVersion number for the Notebook. This is an audit column.   CreatedByThe user who created the Notebook. This is an audit column.  Created OnDate and Time of creation  TagsTags can be used for searching metadataN DescDescription of the NotebookN ActiveActivate / De-Activate the NotebookYYLockedRestricts any modifications to the rule. Can only be unlocked by application admin user.YNPublishedMakes the Notebook visible to other users within the same application.YYPublicMakes the Notebook visible in read only mode to all users across the platform.YNIs TemplateMakes the Notebook visible in read only mode to all users across the platform.N Use TemplateSelect the Use TemplateN Notebook TypeSelect the Notebook Type Jupyter/Zeppelin          Y , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseDashboardDashboardInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAnalytics WorkbenchAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisRelease Notes - 6.1.0Release Notes - 6.1.0Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisModelModelInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDatapodDatapodInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInferyx Release NotesInferyx Release NotesInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence
