title: Install/update inferyx platform - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Install/update inferyx platform - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Install/update inferyx platformBackup Recovery ProcessInferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Install/update inferyx platformMore actionsInstall/update inferyx platformYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Sept 12, 20244 min readdeploy_framework.shThis script is used to install Application  . It provides options for specifying the Database name host name port number mongodb username, mongodb password, application name .Usage: ./deploy_framework.sh [OPTIONS]
Options:
Mandatory Options:
*    -a, --app-name          Application name, comma-separated (e.g., all or admin,edw)
*    -d, --database          MongoDB database name
*    -o, --organization      App organization name
*    -u, --username          MongoDB username
*    -p, --password          MongoDB password
Optional Options:
     -D, --default-app       Set 'no' if the common app is not required (default: yes)
     -s, --server            Select machine type it's a server or local(default: server)
     -H, --host              MongoDB host name (default: localhost)
     -P, --port              MongoDB port number (default: 27017)
     -h, --help              Display this help messageOptions:-a, --app-name Specify the Application name.-d, --database Specify the Database name.-o, --organization Specify the App organization name.-u, --username Specify the mongodb username.-p, --password Specify the mongodb password.-D, --default-app Specify the default-app.-s --server Specify the server.-H, --host Specify the host name.-P, --port Specify the port number.Additionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message.create_indexes.shThis script is used to indexing the mongodb collection  . It provides options for specifying the Database name host name port number mongodb username, mongodb password .Usage: ./create_indexes.sh [OPTIONS]
Options:
*    -d, --database          Database name
*    -H, --host              host name
*    -P, --port              port number
*    -u, --username          mongodb username
*    -p, --password          mongodb password
     -h, --help              Display this help message
Options:-d, --database Specify the Database name.-H, --host Specify the host name.-P, --port Specify the port number.-u, --username Specify the mongodb username.-p, --password Specify the mongodb password.Additionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message.install_application.shThis script is used to install Application with create a folders and files  . It provides options for specifying the Database name host name port number mongodb username, mongodb password, application name and action.Usage: ./install_application.sh [OPTIONS]
Options:
*    -a, --app-name          Application name
*    -A, --action            Select action type.Ex refresh/append
*    -d, --database          Database name
*    -H, --host              host name
*    -P, --port              port number
*    -u, --username          mongodb username
*    -p, --password          mongodb password
     -h, --help              Display this help message
Options:-a, --app-name Specify the Application name.-A, --action Specify the Action Like refresh/append.-d, --database Specify the Database name.-H, --host Specify the host name.-P, --port Specify the port number.-u, --username Specify the mongodb username.-p, --password Specify the mongodb password.Additionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message.update_createdOn.shThis script is used to update the creaton on mongodb collection  . It provides options for specifying the Database name host name port number mongodb username, mongodb password .Usage: ./update_createdOn.sh [OPTIONS]
Options:
*    -d, --database          Database name
*    -H, --host              host name
*    -P, --port              port number
*    -u, --username          mongodb username
*    -p, --password          mongodb password
     -h, --help              Display this help message
* Required parameterOptions:-d, --database Specify the Database name.-H, --host Specify the host name.-P, --port Specify the port number.-u, --username Specify the mongodb username.-p, --password Specify the mongodb password.Additionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message. create_all_db.shThis script is used to  create a mysql databases and tables. It provides options for specifying the Database type  , schema name, username password .Usage: ./create_all_db.sh [OPTIONS]
Options:
*    -o, --org               Inferyx platform organization name
*    -a, --app               Inferyx platform application name
*    -ai, --acc              Snowflake New account id
*    -r, --role              Snowflake account role
*    -fn, --first-name       Snowflake account first name
*    -ln, --last-name        Snowflake account last name
*    -e, --email-id          Snowflake account email id
*    -R, --resion            Snowflake account resion
*    -E, --edition           Snowflake account edition
     -c, --code              Application code (Only required if run from API)
     -i, --app-id            Application id (Only required if run from API)
*    -s, --schema-type       Database schema name with or without (comma seperated) like: admin,cfa or all
*    -d, --database-type     Database type, ex: mysql/sql/mssql/snowflake
*    -D, --database          Snowflake database name.(Required if database type snowflake)
*    -u, --username          Mysql/Snowflake username
*    -p, --password          Mysql password (Required if database type mysql)
*    -A, --account           Snowflake account number.(Required if database type snowflake)
*    -w, --warehouse         Snowflake warehouse name.(Required if database type snowflake)
     -h, --help              Display this help message
* Required parameter
Mysql:-d, --database-type Specify the database type like mysql-s, --schema-type Specify the schema name Like: all/aml-u, --username Specify the mysql username-p, --password Specify the mysql password-o, --org Specify the platform organization name-a, --app Specify the platform application nameAdditionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message.deploy.shThis script is used to  deploy the latest code on the tomcat serverUsage: ./deploy.sh [OPTIONS]
Options:
  -b <backup>:   Put no if backup skip
*  -U <username>: mongodb username
*  -P <******>: mongodb password
   -m <username>: Mysql username
   -n <******>: Mysql password
   -r <redeploy>: If you want to repatch put yes
   -h, --help: Show help
* Required parameter
Options:-U, --mongo-username Specify the  Mongodb username.-P, --mongo-password Specify the  Mongodb password.-m, --mysql-username Specify the  Mysql username.-n, --mysql-password  Specify the Mysql password.-b, --backup Specify the backup if needed.-r, --repatch Specify the repatch if needed.Additionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message., multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Installation GuideInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisInferyx Project SetupInferyx Project SetupInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure MongoInstall and Configure MongoInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisProject  Deployment ProcessProject  Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Backup Recovery Process - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Backup Recovery Process - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualGerting startedOverviewUser ProfileGlobal SearchData DiscoveryData VisualizationData ManagementBusiness Rule EngineAdvanced AnalyticsWorkflow ManagerBatch SchedulerAdministrationAnalytics WorkbenchInferyx Shell Script (Optional)Install/update inferyx platformBackup Recovery ProcessInferyx SDKInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuideYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Backup Recovery ProcessMore actionsBackup Recovery ProcessYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Jul 10, 20235 min readbackup_mongo_execs.shThis  script is used to backup a MongoDB collection with the option to include the CreatedOn ISOdate.Usage: ./backup_mongo_execs.sh [OPTIONS]
Options:
-c, --collection         Collection name
-d, --date               ISO date
-D, --database           Database name
-H, --host               Host name
-P, --port               Port number
-u, --username           MongoDB username
-p, --password           MongoDB password
-h, --help               Display this help message
This script provides various options to customize the backup process:-c, --collection Specify the name of the collection to be backed up.-d, --date Add an ISO date to the backup filename for reference.-D, --database Specify the name of the database containing the collection.-H, --host Provide the hostname or IP address of your MongoDB server.-P, --port Set the port number on which MongoDB is running.-u, --username Use this option if authentication is enabled and provide your username.-p, --password If authentication is enabled and you have a password set for your username, use this option to supply it2. mongodb_backup.shThis  script is used to backup MongoDB databases without exec contain collections name. It provides options for specifying the database name, host name, port number, username, and password.Usage: ./mongodb_backup.sh [OPTIONS]
Options:
*    -d, --database          Database name
*    -H, --host              Host name
*    -P, --port              Port number
*    -u, --username          MongoDB username
*    -p, --password          MongoDB password
     -h, --help              Display this help message
Options:-d or --database: Specify the database name.-H or --host: Specify the host name.-P or --port: Specify the port number.-u or --username: Specify the MongoDB username.-p or --password: Specify the MongoDB password.Additionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message.3. mongodb_restore.shThis  script is used to Restore backup MongoDB databases . It provides options for specifying the database name, host name, port number, username, and password and backup file path.Usage: ./mongodb_restore.sh [OPTIONS]
Options:
*    -d, --database          Database name
*    -H, --host              Host name
*    -P, --port              Port number
*    -u, --username          MongoDB username
*    -p, --password          MongoDB password
*    -f, --file-path         Backup file path
     -h, --help              Display this help message
Options:-d, --database Specify the database name.-H, --host Specify the host name.-P, --port Specify the port number.-u, --username Specify the MongoDB username.-p, --password Specify the MongoDB password.-f, --file-path Specify the MongoDB backup full file pathAdditionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message.4. mysql_backup.shThis  script is used to take a  mysql databases Backup. It provides options for specifying the database name,  username, and password.Usage: ./mysql_backup.sh [OPTIONS]
Options:
*    -d, --database          Database name, e.g., DEFAULT/default
*    -u, --username          MySQL username
*    -p, --password          MySQL password
     -h, --help              Display this help message
Options:-d, --database Specify the mysql database name -u, --username Specify the mysql username -p, --password Specify the mysql password Additionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message.5. mysqldb_restore.shThis  script is used to take a  mysql databases restore. It provides options for specifying the database name,  username, and password.Usage: ./mysqldb_restore.sh [OPTIONS]
Options:
*    -d, --database          Database name, e.g., DEFAULT/default/framework_admin
*    -D, --date              Backup created date-time
*    -u, --username          MySQL username
*    -p, --password          MySQL password
     -h, --help              Display this help message
Options:-d, --database Specify the mysql database name -u, --username Specify the mysql username -p, --password Specify the mysql password Additionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message6. purge_mongo_execs.shThis  script is used to  purge MongoDB collections . It provides options for specifying the database name, host name, port number, username, password, collection name and ISOdate  .Usage: ./purge_mongo_execs.sh [OPTIONS]
Options:
*    -c, --collection        Collection name
*    -D, --date              Iso date
*    -d, --database          Database name
*    -H, --host              Host name
*    -P, --port              Port number
*    -u, --username          MongoDB username
*    -p, --password          MongoDB password
     -h, --help              Display this help message
 Options:-d, --database Specify the database name.-H, --host Specify the host name.-P, --port Specify the port number.-u, --username Specify the MongoDB username.-p, --password Specify the MongoDB password.-c, --collection Specify the MongoDB collection name-D, --darw Specify the MongoDB collection document createdOn date.Additionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message.7. update_config.shThis  script is used to  white labeling of inferyx project .Usage: ./update_config.sh [OPTIONS]
Options:
     -a, --about             About us
*    -b, --brand             Company alias name
*    -c, --company           Company name
     -s, --secret            Google client id
     -l,  --link             Website link
    -il, --logo-image        Logo image name with .png extension
    -ib, --logo-black        Logo black name with .png extension
    -iw, --logo-white        Logo white name with .png extension
    -it, --logo-transparent  Logo transparent name with .png extension
     -f, --favicon           Favicon
**   -p, --path              Image path (-p/--path) is required when using logo options
     -h, --help              Display this help message
 Options:-a, --about Specify the aboutUs of company.-b, --brand Specify Company alias name.-c, --company Specify company name.-s, --secret Specify the Google client id.-l, --link Specify the company link.--il, --logo-image Specify the logo image name with .png extension-ib, --logo-black Specify the logo black name with .png extension-iw, --logo-white Specify the logo white name with .png extension-it, --logo-transparent Specify the logo transparent name with .png extension-f, --favicon Specify theFavicon.-p, --path Specify the Image path (-p/--path) is required when using logo optionsAdditionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message.8. deploy.shThis  script is used to  deploy the inferyx project on tomcat server Project auto deployment
deploy.sh
Usage: ./deploy.sh [-b <build-type>] [-U <mongodb-username>] [-P <mongodb-password>] [-u <system-username>] [-p <system-password>] [-m <mysql-username>] [-n <mysql-password>] [-v <verbose>] [-h]
Options:
  -b <final>: --Build type
  -U <mongodb-username>: --MongoDB username
  -P <mongodb-password>: --MongoDB password
  -u <system-username>: --System user
  -p <system-password>: --System password
  -m <mysql-username>: --MySQL username
  -n <mysql-password>: --MySQL password
  -v <verbose>: --Verbose mode (0 or 1)
  -h, --help: Show help
 Options:-U  Specify the MongoDB username.-P  Specify the MongoDB password.-u  Specify the system username-p  Specify the system password-m  Specify the mysql username (Optional)-n  Specify the mysql password (Optional).-v  Specify the version number-b  Specify the build typeAdditionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message. 9. deploy_platform.shThis  script is used to  deploy the inferyx project from jenkins to server Usage: ./deploy_platform.sh [-b <build-type>] [-c <client_id>] [-v <version>] [-i <ip address> ] [-u <system user> ] [-p <system password> ] [-m <mongodb username>] [-p <mongodb password>] [-n <brand name> ] [-l <href link>] [-h]
Options:
  -b <final/test>: --Build Type
  -c <client_id>: --Google client id 
  -v <version>: --Version mode (0 or 1)
  -i <ip>: --Server IP address
  -u <system_user>: --System username
  -p <system_password>: --System password
  -U <mongodb_user>: --MongoDB username
  -P <mongodb_password>: --MongoDB password
  -n <brand_name>: --Brand name
  -l <href_link>: --Website link
  -h, --help: Show help
 Options:-U  Specify the MongoDB username.-P  Specify the MongoDB password.-u  Specify the system username-p  Specify the system password-i  Specify the server ip address-n  Specify the company name-l  Specify the company website url-v  Specify the version number-b  Specify the build type-c  Specify the google client id. if you need to configure sso.Additionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message.purge_collection.shThis  script is used to  purge MongoDB collections . It provides options for specifying the database name, host name, port number, username, password, collection name and date or date range .Usage: ./purge_mongo_execs.sh [OPTIONS]
Options:
*    -c, --collection        Collection name
*    -d, --database          Database name
     -D, --date		         CreatedOn Date (dd-mm-yyyy) format or Range of date. Like:30 
*    -H, --host              host name
*    -P, --port              port number
*    -u, --username          mongodb username
*    -p, --password          mongodb password
     -h, --help              Display this help message
 Options:-c, --collection Specify the collection name.-d, --database Specify the database name.-D, --date Specify the createdon date or date range.-H, --host Specify the host name.-P, --port Specify the port number.-u, --username Specify the MongoDB username.-p, --password Specify the MongoDB password.Additionally, you can use the option "-h" or "--help" to display this help message. Please note that the options and parameter values provided in the documentations are placeholders and should be replaced with the actual values you intend to use when executing the scripts. , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseStepsStepsInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisProject White-lable ProcessProject White-lable ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInstallation and Configure Studio 3TInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure MongoInstall and Configure MongoInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Fresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.x - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:
Fresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.x - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuidePre-requisitesFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Fresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xMore actionsFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Feb 05, 20253 min readThis document outlines the steps to download, extract, and install the Inferyx Platform. It includes the necessary commands to execute the installation and database setup scripts. Request the Download FileChoose one of the below options to download the patch file.Option 1: Using curlTo obtain the necessary download file for the patch, use the following curl command. This will authenticate and download the required tar file directly to your system.curl https://dev.inferyx.com/download/inferyx-platform-final-v5.4.4.5.tar.gzFile Name: inferyx-platform-final-v5.4.4.5.tar.gzOROption 2: Using scpAlternatively, you can securely copy (scp) the tar file from the remote server. Replace <username>, <remote_host>, and /path/to/ with the appropriate values.scp <username>@<remote_host>:/path/to/inferyx-platform-final-v5.4.4.5.tar.gz .Note: The dot (.) at the end of the command saves the file to your current working directory.Navigate to the DirectoryFirst, navigate to the /install/framework/build/ directory:cd /install/framework/build/ Extract the Tar FileExtract the downloaded tar file into the /install/framework/build/ directory:tar -xvf inferyx-platform-final-v5.4.4.5.tar.gz Navigate to the Deployment Directorycd /install/framework/build/inferyx-platform-test-v5.4.4.5/deploy/bin Option 1: Run the install_framework_7.sh ScriptTo view the available options for the install_framework_7.sh script, run:./install_framework_7.sh -hMandatory Options for Application:-o, --organization: Organization names (comma-separated)-a, --app-name: Application name-d, --mongo-database: MongoDB Database name-u, --username: Database username-p, --mongo-password: Mongo password-D, --default-app: Set 'no' if the common app is not required (default: yes)-S, --server: Select machine type (server or local, default: server)-H, --host: MongoDB host name (default: localhost)-P, --port: MongoDB port number (default: 27017)Mandatory Options for Databases:-mo, --mysql-organization: Organization names (comma-separated)-ma, --mysql-app-name: Application name-s, --schema-type: Database schema name (comma-separated) like: admin,cfa or all-t, --database-type: Database type (e.g., mysql/sql/mssql/snowflake)-mu, --username: Mysql/Snowflake username-mp, --mysql-password: MySQL password (Required if database type is MySQL)-nu, --neo4j-user: Neo4j username-np, --neo4j-pass: Neo4j password-nd, --neo4j-database-name: Neo4j database nameOptional Options for Deployment:-b, --backup: Skip backup if set to no-r, --redeploy: Re-patch if set to yes-h, --help: Display this help messageExample command to run the script:./install_framework_7.sh -o all -a all -d framework -u inferyx -p 20Inferyx19Option 2: Run Manually for Fresh MetadataTo manually run the script for fresh metadata, use the following command:./install_framework_7.sh -o all -a all -d framework -u inferyx -p 20Inferyx19This command will execute the installation process using the application options for organization=all, app-name=all, mongo-database=framework, and the MongoDBRun the create_all_db.sh Script./create_all_db.sh -hOptions:-o, --org: Inferyx platform organization name-a, --app: Inferyx platform application name-s, --schema-type: Database schema name (comma-separated) like: admin,cfa or all-t, --database-type: Database type (e.g., mysql/sql/mssql/snowflake)-u, --username: Mysql/Snowflake username-p, --password: Mysql password (Required if database type is MySQL)-nu, --username: Neo4j username-np, --password: Neo4j password-nd, --neo4j_database: Neo4j database name-ai, --acc: Snowflake New account id-r, --role: Snowflake account role-fn, --first-name: Snowflake account first name-ln, --last-name: Snowflake account last name-e, --email-id: Snowflake account email id-R, --resion: Snowflake account region-E, --edition: Snowflake account edition-D, --database: Snowflake database name (Required if database type is Snowflake)-c, --code: Application code (Required if running from API)-i, --app-id: Application id (Required if running from API)-A, --account: Snowflake account number (Required if database type is Snowflake)-w, --warehouse: Snowflake warehouse name (Required if database type is Snowflake)-h, --help: Display this help messageExample command to run the script:./create_all_db.sh -o common,inferyx -a all -s all -u inferyx -p inferyx -t mysqlRun the upgrade_framework_7.sh Script./upgrade_framework_7.sh -hOptions:-u <username>: MongoDB username-p <******>: MongoDB password-b <backup>: Skip backup if set to no-r <redeploy>: Re-patch if set to yes-D <database>: Repoint MongoDB database-m <username>: MySQL username-n <******>: MySQL password-h, --help: Show help messageExample command to run the script:./upgrade_framework_7.sh -u inferyx -p 20Inferyx19 -b no -r noThe -r option is not mandatory. Use this option only if you need to patch the same version again.Access the PlatformOpen web browser and verify that the application is accessible and you can log in as a user:URL: http://localhost:8080/framework/app/index.html  OR http://<server_ip>/framework/app/index.html This concludes the installation and setup guide for the Inferyx platform. Follow these steps carefully to ensure a smooth installation process. , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xRead with thisInstall and Configure MongoInstall and Configure MongoInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisProject  Deployment ProcessProject  Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisDocker Deployment ProcessDocker Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisPatch Deployment ProcessPatch Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence

title: Patch Process for MongoDB 7.x Only - Inferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x - Confluence
main_content:

SpacesAppsTemplatesCreateInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xAll contentShortcutsActivityActivity This trigger is hiddenContentResults will update as you type.Project SetupInferyx Installation GuideInferyx User ManualInferyx Release NotesMaster Deployment GuidePre-requisitesFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyYou‘re viewing this with anonymous access, so some content might be blocked.CloseInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.x/Patch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyMore actionsPatch Process for MongoDB 7.x OnlyYogesh PalrechaOwned by Yogesh PalrechaLast updated: Feb 14, 20251 min readRequest the Download FileChoose one of the below options to download the patch file.Option 1: Using curlTo obtain the necessary download file for the patch, use the following curl command. This will authenticate and download the required tar file directly to your system.curl https://dev.inferyx.com/download/inferyx-platform-final-v5.4.4.5.tar.gzFile Name: inferyx-platform-final-v5.4.4.5.tar.gzOROption 2: Using scpAlternatively, you can securely copy (scp) the tar file from the remote server. Replace <username>, <remote_host>, and /path/to/ with the appropriate values.scp <username>@<remote_host>:/path/to/inferyx-platform-final-v5.4.4.5.tar.gz .Note: The dot (.) at the end of the command saves the file to your current working directory.Navigate to the Directory First, navigate to the /install/framework/build/ directory:cd /install/framework/build/Extract the tar file into the /install/framework/build/ directory:tar -xvf /install/inferyx-platform-final-v5.4.4.5.tar.gzFile Name - inferyx-platform-test-v5.4.4.5Navigate to the Deployment Directorycd /install/framework/build/inferyx-platform-test-v5.4.4.5/deploy/binRun the upgrade_framework_7.sh Script./upgrade_framework_7.sh -hOptions:-u <username>: MongoDB username-p <******>: MongoDB password-b <backup>: Skip backup if set to no-r <redeploy>: Repatch if set to yes-D <database>: Repoint MongoDB database-m <username>: MySQL username-n <******>: MySQL password-h, --help: Show help messageExample command to run the script:./upgrade_framework_7.sh -u inferyx -p 20Inferyx19 -b no -r noThe -r option is not mandatory. Use this option only if you need to patch the same version again.Access the PlatformOpen web browser and verify that the application is accessible and you can log in as a user:URL: http://localhost:8080/framework/app/index.html                                                  or  http://<server_ip>/framework/app/index.html  , multiple selections available,Related contentMore infoCollapseInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInstall or Upgrade Mongo 7.xInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xFresh Project Deployment with MongoDB 7.xInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisPatch Deployment ProcessPatch Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall and Configure MongoInstall and Configure MongoInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisInstall/update inferyx platformInstall/update inferyx platformInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisProject  Deployment ProcessProject  Deployment ProcessInferyx Platform Documentation - 5.xMore like thisAtlassian Intelligence
